text: “Yɛ woŋee la ka ba da yeli ko la a dakoroŋ noba, ‘Ta ko neɛ’; kyɛ ‘onaŋ soba zaa naŋ wa ko neɛ, ba na di la o sɛrɛɛ.’

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Kyɛ maa meŋ yele korɔ yɛ la, ka neɛzaa suuri wa iri ne o yɔɔ bee o yɔɔpuulee, a seŋ ka ba di o sɛrɛɛ. Ka fõõ wa to fo yɔɔ bee fo yɔɔpuulee, a seŋ ka ba zeŋ ne fo nembɛrɛ niŋesogɔ. Ka fõõ boɔle fo tɔsoba, ‘Neŋlaane ŋa’, a seŋ ka ba de fo lɔɔ eŋ dazugovũũ poɔ.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: bare a fo kyɔɔtaa a Naaŋmen bagmaalkuuri niŋeŋ kyɛ daŋ gaa ka fo ne a fo tɔsoba na te maale taa sɛre, kyɛ ka fo pãã leɛ wa tere a fo kyɔɔtaa.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: Vɛŋ ka fo ne neɛ naŋ taa yelbawontaa leɛ maale taa wieouŋ lɛ, ka yɛnee naŋ wa be a sobiriŋ gɛrɛ a sɛrɛɛdiibu zie. Ka lɛnaane o na te de fo la ko a sɛredire, ka a sɛredire meŋ leɛ de fo ko a polisiri ka ba de fo lɔɔ eŋ nempage die poɔ.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Yelmeŋɛ ka N yele korɔ fo, fo koŋ yi a nempagdie poɔ, te ta lɛ saŋ fo naŋ yɔɔ a sane zaa baare. Peɛ̃mo Toma Yɛlɛ

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: “Ba da yelee la meŋ, ‘Dɔɔ zaa naŋ wa boɔrɔ ka o bare o pɔge, a seŋɛɛ la ka o sɛge pɔg-bare gane ko o kyɛ bare o.’

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Laboriŋ, yɛ woŋee la ka ba da yeli ko la a dakoroŋ noba, ‘Yɛ ta wa poɔrɔ pɔ-zirii, kyɛ yɛ maŋ iri a pɔɛ zaa yɛ naŋ pɔ ko a Daana.’

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Yɛ vɛŋ ka yɛ yelbie waa, ‘Õõ, Õõ’ bee ‘Ai, Ai.’ A ama puoriŋ, yeli zaa yɛ naŋ wa yele dɔglɔ yi la a sebɛlbɛllɛ zie.’ Taaba Sane Yaabo Yɛlɛ (Luk

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: Yɛ woŋee la ka ba da yeli, ‘Ka neɛŋ wa iri fo nimiri ka fo meŋ iri o meŋ nimiri, bee ka ka neɛŋ wa kɔɔ fo nyenee, meŋ kɔɔ o meŋ nyenee.’

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Kyɛ maa yele korɔ yɛ la: Ta yɔɔ neɛ naŋ e fo faa ne faaloŋ. Kyɛ ka neɛ wa va fo duluŋ lampɛgreŋ, leɛ a gɔɔ meŋ ko o ka o fale;

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: ka neɛzaa wa boɔrɔ ka o sɛ fo naayiri poɔ, a de fo kparbile, de a fo kpar-kpoŋ meŋ ko o poɔ.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Ka neɛzaa meŋ wa fere fo ka fo tuo o boma kyɛŋ ne mɛle yeni, tuo kyɛŋ ne mɛlɛ ayi.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Yɛ maŋ ko neɛzaa naŋ sɔre bon yɛ zie. Yɛ ta wa mɔŋnɔ neɛ naŋ boɔrɔ pɛmmo yɛ zie. Dɔndɔnne Nɔmmo Yɛlɛ (Luk

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Yɛ da woŋee la ka ba yeli, ‘Nɔŋ fo tɔsoba, kyɛ kyiiri fo dɔndɔmɔ.’

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Kyɛ maa yele korɔ yɛ, yɛ nɔŋ yɛ dɔndɔnne, a puoro korɔ banaŋ naŋ dɔgrɔ yɛ,

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: ka yɛ na toɔ̃ waa a yɛ Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ biiri. Bonso, ona la maŋ e ka ŋmenaa puri eŋ a nenfaare ane a nemmenne; a kyɛ e ka saa mi eŋ a nemmenne ane a nenfaare zaa.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Ka yɛneŋ nɔŋ a banaŋ naŋ nɔŋ yɛ yoŋ, boŋ tɔnɔ ka yɛ na nyɛ? A zuyɔɔ-derebɛ meŋ gba nɔnɔ la ba nɔneba wɛ!

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Ka yɛneŋ puoro yɛ yɔɔmine ane yɛ yɔɔpuuli yoŋ, boŋ yeli kpoŋ la ka yɛ erɛ gaŋ yɛ taaba? A Nemboore-yobo meŋ ba maŋ erɛ a lɛ be?

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Azuiŋ yɛ e noba naŋ tori aŋa a yɛ Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ naŋ tori lɛ.”

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Yɛ maaleŋ erɛ a ta wa tonɔ yɛ tonveɛle noba niŋeŋ ka ka ba nyɛ. Ka yɛnee wa erɛ ka noba nyɛ, a yɛ Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ koŋ la yɔɔ yɛ.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Kyɛ ka yɛnee maŋ wa korɔ nembaalba sommo, yɛ ta maŋ vɛŋ ka yɛ nugɔɔ baŋ bonna yɛ nuduluŋ naŋ erɛ,

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: Ka yɛnee maŋ wa puoro, yɛ ta maŋ erɛ aŋa a bampuori-kombampoɔ mine naŋ maŋ erɛ a lɛ. Ba maŋ nɔŋ ka ba are puoro a puoruuderi pooreŋ ane a sobɛrɛ zu ka noba na nyɛ ba. Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, ba de la a ba san-yɔɔ baare.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Kyɛ ka yɛnee maŋ wa puoro, yɛ maŋ kpɛ yɛ deri poore, a page a pama, kyɛ puori a yɛ Saa naŋ be zisɔglaa poɔ; a yɛ Saa naŋ nyɛrɛ a yelsɔglɔ zaa na yɔɔ yɛ la.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Ka yɛnee maŋ wa puoro; yɛ ta maŋ yele yɛlɛ naŋ ba taa tɛgɛ dɔglɔ taa, aŋa a Nemboore-yobo naŋ maŋ erɛ a lɛ. Ba maŋ teɛre ka, ka ba naŋ yele yelyaga, lɛ ka Naaŋmen na woŋ a ba puoruu.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Yɛ ta tɔglɔ ba, bonso, a yɛ Saa maŋ daŋ baŋ la bon yɛ naŋ boɔrɔ ka yɛ naŋ ba sɔre O sɛre.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: “Azuiŋ yɛ maŋ puori ŋaa: Te Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ, ka fo yuori waa soŋ,

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Ka fo naaloŋ wa. Ka fo poboɔbo nyɛ eebo a teŋɛzu, a seŋ a naŋ waa lɛ tenvelaa poɔ.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Ka yɛnee dire yɛ taaba yelfaare suuri korɔ ba, a yɛ Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ na di la yɛ meŋ yelfaare suuri ko yɛ.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Kyɛ ka yɛnee ba dire yɛ taaba yelfaare suuri korɔ ba, a yɛ Saa meŋ koŋ di yɛ meŋ yelfaare suuri ko yɛ. Nɔleŋ Yɛlɛ

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: ka lɛ vɛŋ ka noba ta baŋ ka yɛ leŋ la noɔre. Kyɛ a yɛ Saa naŋ nyɛrɛ yelsɔglɔ zaa na ko yɛ la a san-yɔɔ. Tenvelaa Bondaanoŋ Yɛlɛ (Luk

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Yɛ moɔ nimiri a daŋ bɔ a Naaŋmen naaloŋ ane O mennoŋ, kyɛ O pãã na de la a boma ama zaa naŋ kyɛre meŋ ko yɛ.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Azuiŋ, yɛ ta kyerɛ bieu yɛlɛ, bonso, a bieu meŋ taa la o meŋɛ deme. Bebiri zaa taa la o meŋɛ yɛlɛ.

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Yɛ ta boɔrɔ yɛ bondaaboma binne a teŋɛzu kyɛ, benee mɔgre ane zoɔ̃ naŋ na di a, bee nannyigri naŋ na kɔɔ a pane kyɛ kpɛ zu a;

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: kyɛ yɛ boɔrɔ yɛ bondaaboma binne tenvelaa poɔ, be ka mɔgre bee zoɔ̃ kontoɔ̃ di a, bee ka nannyigri kontoɔ̃ kɔɔ a pane kyɛ kpɛ zu a.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Bonso, zie na fo bondaaboma naŋ be, be la ka fo sukyiri meŋ na be. Nimiri La A Eŋɛ Veɛloŋ (Luk

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Kyɛ ka fo nimiri wa sãã, a fo eŋɛ zaa na paale ne la ligruŋ, kyɛ ka a veɛloŋ naŋ be fo eŋɛ wa e ligruŋ, nyɛ lɛ a ligruŋ naŋ pãã na e kpoŋ seŋ! Nembɛrɛ Bayi Toma Tommo Yɛlɛ (Luk

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Neɛzaa kontoɔ̃ tonɔ korɔ nembɛrɛ bayi. O na kyiiri la kaŋ, kyɛ nɔŋ kaŋ; bee o na sagra la kaŋ noɔre kyɛ zagra kaŋ deni. Yɛ kontoɔ̃ tonɔ korɔ Naaŋmen kyɛ naŋ kpimare libi-bɔ meŋ. Yɛ Ta Kyerɛ (Luk

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Azuiŋ, yɛ ta kyerɛ a yele, ‘Boŋ ka te na di?’ bee ‘Boŋ ka te na nyu?’ bee ‘Boŋ ka te na su?’

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: “Azuiŋ, N yele korɔ yɛ la, yɛ ta vɛŋ ka yɛ nyɔvore yɛlɛ dɔɔnɔ yɛ, bon yɛ naŋ na di bee a nyu, bee ka bonsuuri yɛlɛ dɔɔnɔ yɛ. A nyɔvore ba gaŋ a bondiraa be, ka a eŋɛ meŋ gaŋ a bonsuuri be?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Yɛ leɛ kaa nyɛ a sazu bon-ɛgre, a ba borɔ bee a kyeɛrɛ, bee eŋnɛ boma bugri poɔ meŋ; kyɛ a yɛ tenvelaa Saa guolo a la. Yɛ ba seɛ a bon-ɛgre ama yaga zaa be?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: Ka bonso, ka yɛ kyerɛ bonsuuri yɛlɛ? Yɛ kaa nyɛ a baa pɛmpɛllɛ naŋ maŋ baara lɛ. A ba tonɔ toma bee a wuoro pɛmɛ,

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: kyɛ anealɛzaa, N yele korɔ yɛ la, haali gba Solomɔn ne o naaloŋ veɛloŋ zaa daŋ ba su bonsuuri ka a veɛlɛ seŋ a ama kanzaa.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Kyɛ ka Naaŋmen pãã kaara a weɛ moɔ zu seŋ ŋaa, anaŋ are zenɛ ka zie wa nyaana ka ba de lɔɔ eŋ vũũ poɔ, O koŋ kaa yɛ a gaŋ a lɛ be, yɛnee yelde-fẽẽ deme?

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: A Nemboore-yobo la yɔ zoro boɔrɔ a teŋɛzu boma ama. Serɛzaa, a yɛ Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ baŋ ka yɛ boɔrɔ la a boma ama zaa.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: “Ta di fo tɔ sɛrɛɛ, ka ba ta wa di fo meŋ sɛrɛɛ.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: A lɛ fo naŋ di fo tɔ sɛrɛɛ, lɛ ka ba na di fo meŋ sɛrɛɛ. Bonna fo naŋ manne ne ko fo tɔsoba, a bonna ne o zu ka ba na manne ne ko fo meŋ.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: Bonso ka fo nyɛ muri naŋ be fo tɔsoba nimiri poɔ, kyɛ ba nyɛ a wɛge naŋ be fo meŋɛ nimiri poɔ?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Bee wola ka fo na toɔ̃ yeli ko fo tɔsoba, ‘Vɛŋ ka N iri muri bare fo nimiri poɔ,’ kyɛ ka wɛge be fo meŋɛ nimiri poɔ?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Fõõ bampuori-kombampoɔ ŋa, daŋ iri a wɛge naŋ be fo meŋɛ nimiri poɔ bare, lɛ ka fo na nyɛrɛ velaa a pãã toɔ̃ iri a muri bare fo tɔsoba nimiri poɔ. Ta Dɛge Bon Naŋ E Soŋ

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Ka yɛnee gba naŋ waa nenfaare baŋ ka yɛ de bonsoŋ ko yɛ bibiiri, wola ka yɛ Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ koŋ ko banaŋ zaa naŋ maŋ sɔre bonveɛle O zie gaŋ lɛ!

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Nembuo la be yɛ poɔ kyɛ, ka o bie na sɔre boroboro ka o de kuuri ko o?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Yɛ sɔre, ba na ko yɛ la; yɛ bɔ, yɛ na nyɛ la, yɛ kpaare a pane, ba na yuo ko yɛ la ka yɛ kpɛ.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Bonso, neɛzaa naŋ maŋ sɔre ba maŋ ko o la, neɛzaa meŋ naŋ maŋ bɔ o maŋ nyɛ la, ka a pane maŋ yuo ko neɛzaa meŋ naŋ kpaare.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Bee ka o bie na sɔre zommo, ka o de waabo ko o?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Yelzaa poɔ, yɛ maŋ erɛ korɔ yɛ taaba a seŋ lɛ yɛ naŋ boɔrɔ ka ba erɛ korɔ yɛ; bonso, a begɛ ane a Ŋmen-yelmanneba wuluu tɛgɛ la a lɛ. A Dendɔ-fogloo Yɛlɛ (Luk

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: “Neɛ na zaa naŋ woŋ a N yelbie ama, a erɛ seŋ lɛ a naŋ wuli, na waa seŋ ŋa dɔɔ yɛmbaŋna kaŋ naŋ mɛ o yiri piiri zu.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Kyɛ a nyɔvore dendɔre waa la fogloo, ka o tuubu waa tuo, ka noba fẽẽ tuuro o. Teɛ Ane O Wɔmɔ Yɛlɛ (Luk

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: “Yɛ baŋna ne a kaŋwaa Ŋmen-yelmanneba yɛlɛ. Ba maŋ pilli la pegama a wa yɛ zie kyɛ ka ba pooreŋ ka ba waa gboŋgboe.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Ba yel-erre la na wuli noba na ba naŋ waa. Ba mɔɔ maŋ pɔre la zemberemɛ gɔteere zu bee a pɔre kaŋkanne gɔzaŋaa teɛ zu?

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: A lɛ ne o zu, teɛ zaa naŋ waa velaa maŋ wɔŋ la wɔŋ veɛle; ka teɛ naŋ waa faa meŋ wɔŋ wɔŋ faare.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Teɛ naŋ waa velaa kontoɔ̃ wɔŋ wɔŋ faare, bee ka teɛ naŋ waa faa wɔŋ wɔŋ veɛle.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Teɛ na zaa naŋ ba wɔnɔ wɔŋ veɛle, ba na kyɛ o la a lɔɔ eŋ vũũ poɔ.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Azuiŋ yɛ na baŋ ba la a yi ba yel-erre zie. Meŋɛ Bɛlloo Yɛlɛ (Luk

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Neɛ na zaa meŋ naŋ woŋ a N yelbie ama, kyɛ ba erɛ seŋ lɛ a naŋ wuli, na waa seŋ ŋa dɔɔ damboli kaŋ naŋ mɛ o yiri biiree zu.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Ka saa mi, ka kɔmpie ŋmɛ, ka saseɛ fu dɔ̃ɔ̃ a yiri ka o zaa le vɔgle teŋɛ muro muro. A leebu da sereŋ waa la kpoŋ yaga!”

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “A ba e ka neɛzaa naŋ boɔlɔ ma ‘Daana, Daana,’ la na kpɛ a tenvelaa naaloŋ. Kyɛ soba na yoŋ naŋ erɛ a N Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ poboɔbo yɛlɛ la na kpɛ a tenvelaa

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: A bebiri na daare, noba yaga na yeli ko ma la, ‘Daana, Daana, te mɔɔ naane da moɔlɔ a Naaŋmen yelbiri a fo yuori eŋɛ, a da digre sefaare fo yuoriŋ, a kyɛ da tonɔ toŋkpeɛne yaga a fo yuori eŋɛŋ?’

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Kyɛ N pãã na leɛ yeli ko ba la, ‘Maa ba baŋ yɛ togitogi; yɛ tage yi be N zie, yɛnee yelfa-erebɛ ŋa.’ A Piiri Zu Yiri Ane A Biiree Zu Yiri Yɛlɛ (Luk

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Ka saa mi, ka kɔmpie ŋmɛ, ka saseɛ fu dɔ̃ɔ̃ a yiri kyɛ ka o ba le, bonso, piiri zu la ka o biŋ a yiri pare.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa yeli a yɛlɛ ama zaa baare, a nembeɛoŋ nɔɛ da maaɛ la ne a O wuluu,

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: bonso, O da kanne ba la aŋa neɛ naŋ taa kpeɛ̃õ. O ne a Naaŋmen bege wulwulibo kannoo da ba yi taa.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: kyɛ ka ba de a naaloŋ gbandiribe lɔɔ eŋ lige poɔ, ka ba be a be a kono nyerɛ nyemɛ.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: A zimaane na, ba da taa la noba yaga sefaare naŋ be ba enne a wa ne a Yeezu zie, ka O da yeli yelbi-yenaa yoŋ a digi a sefaare ka a yi, kyɛ da saŋ banaŋ zaa naŋ da beɛrɛ.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: ka kɔnkɔŋɔ kaŋ da wa gbi dumo O niŋesogɔ, a kyɛ yele, “Daana, ka fõõ boɔrɔ, fo na toɔ̃ saŋ ma la.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Ka Yeezu da teɛ O nu a seene o, kyɛ yele, “N boɔrɔ la, nyɛ fo sammo.” A tɔntɔbaalɛ ka a dɔɔ kɔnnoŋ deɛ da baare.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.

ref: Jesus went back to Nazareth with his parents and obeyed them. His mother kept on thinking about all that had happened.

ref: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”

text: Yɛ wɔŋ wɔnveɛle naŋ na wuli ka yɛ sereŋ leɛ la yɛ eebo. Yɛ ta piili yele korɔ yɛmenne, ‘Aboraham la te sããkoma’; maa yele korɔ yɛ, Naaŋmen na toɔ̃ e la ka kubo ama leɛ biiri a ko Aboraham.

ref: But to Herod the ruler, he said, “It was wrong for you to take Herodias, your brother's wife.” John also said Herod had done many other bad things.

ref: He said to them: A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.
text: O da yeli ko ba la, “A weɛ boma maalɛɛ la yaga, kyɛ ka a tontonema ba seŋ bon, azuiŋ, yɛ sɔre a weɛ boma Daana ka o toŋ tontonema ka ba gaa te de a weɛ boma.

ref: Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves.

ref: Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road.

ref: As soon as you enter a home, say, “God bless this home with peace.”

ref: If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you.

ref: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
text: Yɛ saŋna a baalba naŋ be a be, kyɛ yeli ko ba, ‘A Naaŋmen naaloŋ wa peɛle yɛ la.’

text: Kyɛ fõõ Kapanum, fo teɛre ka ba na zɛge fo la do ne saa be? Ai, ba na sigri fo la te eŋ dazugovũũ poɔ.”

ref: Jesus told them: I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.

ref: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
text: Yɛ nyɛ, N ko yɛ la kpeɛ̃õ ka yɛ na neɛrɛ weere ane nanne, kyɛ taa faŋ a dɔndɔmɔ kpeɛ̃õ zaa zu, ka yelfaa zaa koŋ pɔge yɛ.

ref: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”

ref: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
text: Ka a dɔɔ yeli sɔge, “Nɔŋ a Daana fo Naaŋmen ne fo sukyiri zaa, ane fo seɛ zaa, ane fo faŋ zaa, ane fo yɛŋ zaa, kyɛ nɔŋ fo tɔ a seŋ lɛ fo naŋ nɔŋ fo meŋɛ.”

ref: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: A gogo na poɔŋ, Yeezu da taa la popeɛloŋ a Vooroŋ Soŋ poɔ a pãã yeli, “N Saa, a sazu ne teŋɛ Daana, N puoro fo la bareka ne fo naŋ sɔgle a yɛlɛ ama a yɛndeme ane baŋnema zie, kyɛ iri a wuli bilɛɛre. Õõ, N Saa, a fo poboɔbo sereŋ la a lɛ.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: A N Saa de la a boma zaa ko ma. Neɛzaa ba baŋ neɛ na a Bidɔɔ naŋ waa, ka a Saa yoŋ naane. Neɛzaa meŋ ba baŋ neɛ na a Saa naŋ waa, ka a Bidɔɔ yoŋ naane ane banaŋ a Bidɔɔ naŋ boɔrɔ ka O de a Saa wuli ba.”

ref: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!

ref: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge o, “Dɔɔ kaŋ la da yi Gyɛruzɛlɛm a sigre a Gyɛreko teŋɛ. Nannyig-kpeɛne mine da yi ŋmaa la a dɔɔ ŋa a faa o boma zaa, a yagre o bonsuuri bare a kyɛ ŋmɛ o gbɛre kyɛ te gɛrɛ.

ref: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
text: Ka Naaŋmen bagmaala kaŋ meŋ la lɛ a da tu a sori na waana, kyɛ o naŋ da wa nyɛ a dɔɔ naŋ gaŋ, o da bale o la bare kyɛ te gɛrɛ.

ref: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
text: A lɛ ne o zu la ka Levi boorɔ neɛ kaŋ meŋ te wa ta a zie na a dɔɔ naŋ gaŋ, ka o da bale a dɔɔ baala ŋa bare kyɛ te gɛrɛ.

ref: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
text: Kyɛ Samareya dɔɔ kaŋ meŋ da tu la a sori na waana, a wa nyɛ a dɔɔ ka o gaŋ, ka o nembaaloŋ zaa kpɛ o.

ref: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.

ref: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”

ref: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
text: Ka a nɔbaŋna yeli, “A soba na naŋ zo o nembaaloŋ.” Ka a Yeezu yeli o, “Meŋ gaa te erɛ a lɛ.” Yeezu Naŋ Gaa Maata Ne Mareya Zie

ref: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.

text: yeli yenaa yoŋ la ferɛ. A bonvelaa la ka Mareya kaa-iri, ka ba koŋ baŋ faa o yi o zie.”

text: “Lɛnso ka N yele korɔ yɛ, Yɛ sɔre, ba na ko yɛ la, yɛ bɔ, yɛ na nyɛ la, yɛ kpaare a pane, ba na yuo ko yɛ la ka yɛ kpɛ.

text: Ka yɛnee gba naŋ waa nenfaare baŋ ka yɛ de bonveɛle ko yɛ bibiiri, bonso, ka a yɛ Saa naŋ be a tenvelaa poɔ koŋ ko banaŋ zaa naŋ sɔre a Vooroŋ Soŋ yi O zie.” Yeezu Ane Bɛlezɛbu Yɛlɛ (Mat

ref: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.

ref: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
text: Yɛ yuori la we, Faraziimine! Yɛ maŋ nɔŋ ka yɛ zeŋ a nembɛrɛ zenziiri a puoruuderiŋ, kyɛ nɔnɔ ka noba puoro yɛ ne emmo a dare pooreŋ.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”

ref: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
text: O pãã maŋ leɛ gaa la, a te taa o taaba mine ayopoĩ naŋ waa faare gaŋ o meŋɛ wa ne, ka a zaa wa kpeɛrɛ be. A neɛ ŋa soba yɛlɛ pãã maŋ maaleŋ eɛɛ faa yaga gaŋ o dɛndɛŋ na.” Maaloo Serɛzaa

ref: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
text: Ka a Yeezu leɛ yeli, “Banaŋ naŋ woŋ a Naaŋmen yelbiri a tuuro a yelbiri la nyɛ maaloo.” A Gyona Yelwulaa (Mat

ref: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
text: A Farazii ŋa noɔre da maaɛ la ne o naŋ nyɛ ka a Yeezu ba pɛge O nuuri kyɛ dire a diibu.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
text: “Kyɛ yɛ yuori la we, Faraziimine! Yɛ maŋ iri la badoge ane nyɛremaanyuu ane zɛva-boorɔ zaa a tere a Naaŋmen kyɔɔtaa kyɛ ba kyɛllɛ mennoŋ ane Naaŋmen nɔmmo yɛlɛ. A ama la ka a seŋ ka yɛ da erɛ a kyɛ ta bare a anaŋ meŋ eebo.

ref: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
text: A noba naŋ da wa te erɛ yaga, Yeezu da piili yele korɔ ba la, “A zenɛ noba kpɛ la faa yaga. Ba maŋ boɔrɔ la ka ba nyɛ yelmannaa, kyɛ ba koŋ nyɛ yelmannaa zaa, ka a Gyona yelmannaa na yoŋ naane.

ref: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
text: A Kɔmpare paaloŋ pɔgenaa ne a zenɛ noba na laŋ iri la a kuuni poɔ a sɛrɛɛdiibu daare, ka o ko ba saanaa. Bonso, o da yi la tateŋɛ wa ka o woŋ a Solomɔn yɛŋ yɛlɛ, kyɛ yɛ nyɛ, neɛ kaŋ naŋ gaŋ Solomɔn be la a kyɛ!

ref: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
text: A Ninevɛ noba ne a zenɛ noba na laŋ iri la a kuuni poɔ a sɛrɛɛdiibu daare, ka Ninevɛ noba ko a zenɛ noba saanaa. Bonso, a Ninevɛ deme da leɛ la ba eebo a saŋ na a Gyona naŋ kanne ba. Kyɛ nyɛ, neɛ kaŋ naŋ gaŋ a Gyona be la a kyɛ! A Eŋɛ Kyaane (Mat

ref: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
text: Neɛzaa ba maŋ kyɔŋ fentelɛ a de gbaale kyigi pɔge. O maŋ de pare la fentel-daa zu ka noba naŋ kpeɛrɛ a die na nyɛrɛ zie.

ref: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
text: A fo nimiri la a fo eŋɛ fentelɛ. Ka a fo nimiri wa taa laafeɛ, fo eŋɛ zaa na paale ne la veɛloŋ. Kyɛ ka a fo nimiriŋ wa sãã, a fo eŋɛ zaa na paale ne la ligruŋ.

ref: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
text: Yɛnee dambolo ŋa! A neɛ na soba naŋ maale a puori, ona naane maale a poɔ meŋ be?

ref: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
text: Yɛ yuori la we! Bonso, yɛnee la mɛ a Ŋmen-yelmanneba na a yɛ sããkommine naŋ ko yaare.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
text: Yeezu naŋ da naŋ yele a yɛlɛ, a Farazii neɛ kaŋ da wa boɔle O la ka O wa o yiri ka ba di. Lɛ la ka a Yeezu wa kpɛ te zeŋ a diibu zie.

ref: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
text: Lɛ la ka a Yeezu da yeli ko o, “A yɛnee Faraziimine maŋ pɛge la a ŋmane ane a laa puori, kyɛ ka a yɛ poore kpɛ paale ne bombɔ-gaŋne ane potuoloŋ.

ref: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
text: Azuiŋ yɛ de boma naŋ be a yɛ ŋmane ane a laa poore ko nembaalba, kyɛ nyɛ, bonzaa na eɛɛ velaa ko yɛ.”

ref: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
text: Yɛ yuori la we! Yɛ waa ŋa yaare naŋ ba maŋ sãã, ka noba ba baŋ kyɛ maŋ do kyɛnɛ a zu.”

ref: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
text: Ka a Yeezu yeli, “Yɛ meŋ yuori la we, yɛnee bege baŋnema! Yɛ maŋ tuo la noba tuotegrɛ naŋ gaŋ ba faŋ, kyɛ yɛmenne koŋ de nubiri seene ne a tuobu.

ref: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
text: A ŋaa yɛ naŋ e wuli ka yɛ di la danseɛ kyɛ sage a lɛ a yɛ sããkommine naŋ e; bonso, banaŋ da ko a Ŋmen-yelmanneba kyɛ ka yɛ meŋ mɛ a ba yaare.

ref: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
text: A lɛnso ka Naaŋmen yeli O yɛŋ poɔ, ‘N na toŋ la Ŋmen-yelmanneba ane apostoliimine ba zie, ka ba na ko ba mine kyɛ dɔgre ba mine.’

ref: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
text: Azuiŋ a Ŋmen-yelmanneba zẽẽ ba naŋ kyiri, a yi a teŋɛ piiluu saŋ, na do la a zenɛ noba,

ref: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
text: a piili ne Abɛl te tɔ Zakareya ba naŋ da ko a Naaŋmen bagmaalkuuri ane a zisoŋ sogɔŋ. Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, a ba dɔgroŋ na dɔgle la a zenɛ noba zu.

ref: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
text: Yɛ yuori la we, yɛnee bege baŋnema! Bonso, yɛ nyɔge la a bammo die saafebiri de; yɛmenne ba kpeɛrɛ kyɛ la pigi banaŋ meŋ naŋ boɔrɔ kpeɛbo sori.”

ref: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa yi yeŋ, a Naaŋmen bege wulwulibo ane a Faraziimine da piili zɔɔrɔ la yɛlɛ yaga, a soore O yɛlɛ yaga,

ref: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
text: a boɔrɔ ka ba bɛre nyɔge O a O yɛlɛ yeluu poɔ.

ref: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
text: A noba naŋ da wa lantaa a te ta ŋa turi ne dɔglɔ, a neɛrɛ taa gbɛɛ la ka Yeezu da piili yele korɔ a O potuuribo, “Yɛ gu yɛmenne velaa a yi a Faraziimine gagarre yɛlɛ poɔ. A ba bampuori-kombampoɔ yɛlɛ la ka N yele.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
text: Kyɛ ka Naaŋmen pãã kaara a weɛ moɔ zu seŋ ŋaa, anaŋ are zenɛ, ka zie wa nyaana ka a kpɛ vũũ, bonso, ka O koŋ kaara yɛ zu gaŋ lɛ, yɛnee yelde-fẽẽ deme!

ref: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
text: Yɛ moɔ nimiri boɔrɔ a Naaŋmen naaloŋ, kyɛ O na ko yɛ la a boma na zaa yɛ naŋ boɔrɔ.”

ref: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
text: N bamine, maa yele korɔ yɛ, yɛ ta zoro banaŋ naŋ maŋ ko a eŋɛ kyɛ ba la taa faŋ na e bonzaa a puoriŋ.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
text: A yɛ zukɔɔloŋ zaa gba noɔre ka ba sɔre. Yɛ ta zoro dabeɛ̃, bonso, yɛ gaŋ la a daviime.

ref: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
text: “N noba, yɛ ta zoro dabeɛ̃, bonso, a yɛ Saa Naaŋmen poboɔbo la ka O ko yɛ a O naaloŋ.

ref: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
text: Yɛ koɔre yɛ boma a de a libie ko nembaalba. Yɛ maale libi-woore na naŋ koŋ sãã ko yɛmenne, a boɔrɔ tenvelaa naboma naŋ ba baara, be ka nannyige zaa kontoɔ̃ wa zu a, ka danduli zaa kontoɔ̃ di a.

ref: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
text: Bonso, a Vooroŋ Soŋ na wuli yɛ la bonna yɛ naŋ na yeli a saŋ na poɔ.” A Bondaa-damboli Sekpɔgre

ref: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
text: Kyɛ ka Naaŋmen yeli ko a dɔɔ, ‘Damboli ŋa! A tensogɔ ŋa zaa ka N na de a fo nyɔvore. Ka a boma fo naŋ nare biŋ, aŋ pãã na so a?’

ref: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
text: Maa yeli ko yɛ! Neɛzaa naŋ sage de N yɛlɛ noba niŋesogɔ, a Nensaala Bie meŋ na sage de o la a Naaŋmen malekere niŋesogɔ.

ref: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
text: Kyɛ soba na zaa naŋ zagre ma bare noba niŋesogɔ, N meŋ na zagre o la bare Naaŋmen malekere niŋesogɔ.

ref: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
text: Ka ba naŋ wa taa yɛ kpɛ ne a puoruuderi ka ba di yɛ sɛrɛɛ, bee a gaa ne yɛ a namine ane a teŋɛ nembɛrɛ zie, yɛ ta miine ne lɛ yɛ naŋ na yeli faa yɛmenne, bee bon yɛ naŋ na yeli sɔge ba.

ref: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
text: Neɛ kaŋ naŋ da be a noba poɔ da yeli ko la a Yeezu, “Wulwullo, vɛŋ ka N yɔɔ e ka te poŋ a te gbandi-boma.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
text: Ka a Yeezu yeli ko o, “N ba, aŋ la biŋ ma ka N e a yɛ sɛredire bee a yɛ bompono?”

ref: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
text: Ka O yeli ko ba, “Yɛ gu yɛmenne velaa a yi bombɔ yaga eŋɛ, bonso, nensaala nyɔvore ba kye o bontaa yaga poɔ.”

ref: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
text: Ka o pãã teɛre o poɔŋ, ‘Boŋ ka N na e, N naŋ ba taa zie a na biŋ a weɛ boma?’

ref: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
text: Lɛ la ka o pãã da yeli, ‘Ŋaa ka N na e. N na ŋmɛre la N bugri ane katanne bare kyɛ leɛ mɛ bonkponni a de a bombie ane n boma eŋ.’

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
text: A Yeezu da yeli ko la O potuuribo, “Azuiŋ N yele korɔ yɛ la, yɛ ta vɛŋ ka yɛ nyɔvore yɛlɛ dɔɔnɔ yɛ, bon yɛ naŋ na di, bee ka bonsuuri yɛlɛ dɔɔnɔ yɛ.

ref: Life is more than food or clothing.
text: Bonso, nyɔvore gaŋ la bondiraa, ka a eŋɛ meŋ gaŋ bonsuuraa.

ref: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
text: Yɛ nyɛ a baa pɛmpɛllɛ naŋ maŋ baara lɛ, a ba tonɔ toma bee a wuoro pɛmɛ, kyɛ anealɛzaa, N yele korɔ yɛ la, haali gba Solomɔn ne o naaloŋ veɛloŋ zaa, o daŋ ba su bonsuuri ka a veɛlɛ seŋ ama kanzaa.

ref: Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink.
text: Yɛ ta dɔɔnɔ yɛmenne, bee a kyerɛ bon yɛ naŋ na di bee a nyu.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
text: Bonso a teŋɛzu noba la maŋ yɔ zoro boɔrɔ a boma ama zaa, kyɛ a yɛ Saa Naaŋmen baŋ ka yɛ boɔrɔ la a boma ama.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
text: Kyɛ ka a gbaŋgbaa wa yeli ko o meŋɛ, ‘N daana kɔɔrɛɛ waabo’, kyɛ pãã piili ŋmeɛrɛ a o gbaŋgba-taaba, a dɔbɔ ane a pɔgeba zaa, kyɛ di boma a nyu dãã te kuoli.

ref: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
text: Yɛ teɛre ka maaroŋ la ka N wa ne a teŋɛzu be? Ai, maa yeli yɛ, kyataa la ka N wa ne!

ref: Be ready and keep your lamps burning
text: “Yɛ nare gu a toma, kyɛ kyɔŋ yɛ fentelɛ.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
text: A gbaŋgbaare banaŋ ba daana naŋ wa nyɛ ba ka ba ba gbiri nyɛ la maaloo. Yelmeŋɛ ka N yele korɔ yɛ, o na nare la o meŋɛ, a e ka ba zeŋ ka o ko ba bondirii.

ref: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
text: Ka o naŋ wa tensogpozentaa bee zinyaane saŋ a wa nyɛ a gbaŋgbaare ka ba be nimiri, ba nyɛ la maaloo.

ref: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
text: Kyɛ yɛ baŋ ka, ka yidandɔɔ da baŋ saŋ na a nannyige naŋ na wa o yiri, o da koŋ sage ka ba kɔɔ a o yiri kpɛ.

ref: So always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come.
text: Yɛ meŋ nare yɛmenne, bonso, a Nensaala Bie na wa la a saŋ na yɛ naŋ ba teɛre ka O na wa la.” Gbaŋgba-bivelaa Bee Gbaŋgba-bifaa Yɛlɛ (Mat

ref: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
text: Piita da soore la Yeezu, “Daana, fo lɔɔ la a sekpɔgre ŋa ko te bee neɛzaa la ka fo lɔɔ tere?”

ref: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: Ka a Daana yeli sɔge, “Aŋ pãã la a tontonɔ na naŋ waa meŋɛ kyɛ baŋ yɛŋ, ka a o daana na e ka o kaara o yiri zu, a na maŋ korɔ o yideme bondirii a terebo saŋ?

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: A gbaŋgbaa na o daana naŋ na leɛ kulo waana a wa nyɛ o ka o tonɔ nyɛ la maaloo.

ref: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, a o daana na e la ka o kaara o boma zaa zu.

ref: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
text: A gbaŋgbaa ŋa daana na wa uri o la a daare na o naŋ ba nare ane gogo na o naŋ ba baŋ. A gbaŋgbaa ŋa daana na wa kyɛ o la ŋmaare puropuro kyɛ de o eŋ a nenfaare poɔ.

ref: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
text: A gbaŋgbaa na naŋ baŋ bonna a o daana naŋ boɔrɔ, kyɛ ba nare o meŋɛ bee a e a lɛ naŋ seŋ, na nyɛ la ŋmeɛroŋ yaga.

ref: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
text: Kyɛ a gbaŋgbaa na naŋ ba baŋ a kyɛ e yɛlɛ naŋ seŋ ka ba ŋmɛ o, na nyɛ la ŋmeɛroŋ belaa lɛ. Neɛzaa ba naŋ ko bon-yaga, ba na boɔrɔ la bon-yaga yi o zie. Neɛzaa ba naŋ de yɛlɛ yaga eŋ o nuuriŋ, ba na boɔrɔ la eroŋ yaga yi o zie.” Yeezu Naŋ Wa Ne Kyataa (Mat

ref: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
text: “N waɛ la ka N mare vũũ eŋ a teŋɛzu, a da berɛ ŋa a vũũ da nyɔge dire la.

ref: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
text: N taa la suobu a na so N meŋɛ, kyɛ N be la toɔrɔ poɔ ka anaŋ ba wa te e baare!

ref: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
text: Ba na kya la taa: Saa na zɔɔrɔ ne la o bie ka bie meŋ zɔɔrɔ ne o saa, ma na zɔɔrɔ ne la o pɔgeyaa ka pɔgeyaa meŋ zɔɔrɔ ne o ma, pɔge na zɔɔrɔ ne la o naapɔge ka naapɔge zɔɔrɔ ne o serɛ ma.” Yɛ Baŋ A Saŋ (Mat

ref: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
text: A Yeezu da la yeli ko la a noba, “Ka yɛnee wa nyɛ zunzugri ka o wa iri a gɛrɛ sapare, yɛ maŋ yeli la daadaalɛ, ‘Saa na wa la’; ka a sereŋ e a lɛ.

ref: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
text: Yɛnee bampuori-kombampoɔ mine! Yɛ maŋ toɔ̃ kaa la a teŋɛ ane a saluoni kyɛ baŋ lɛ a zie naŋ na waa, kyɛ bonso ka yɛ ba toɔ̃ baŋ a zenɛ bebie yɛlɛ eŋ?” Leɛ Maale Taa Yɛlɛ (Mat

ref: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
text: “Ka boŋ la so ka yɛ kontoɔ̃ gyɛle yɛmenne nyɛ lɛ a naŋ seŋ ne?

ref: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
text: Ka neɛ wa taa fo gɛrɛ ne sɛrɛɛdiibu zie, e aneɛ ka yɛ leɛ maale a yɛlɛ a sobiriŋ. Ka lɛ naane, o na tage fo la gaa ne a sɛredire zie, ka a sɛredire de fo ko a polisiri ka ba pɔge fo.

ref: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
text: Maa yele korɔ fo, fo koŋ yi a nempagdie poɔ a te ta lɛ saŋ fo naŋ yɔɔ a sane zaa baare.”

ref: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
text: A saŋ na meŋɛ poɔ la ka noba mine naŋ da be a be da yeli ko a Yeezu lɛnɛɛ Pilato naŋ da ko a Galili noba mine naŋ da maala bagre ka ba zẽẽ ne a donne zẽẽ da pulli taa.

ref: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
text: Ka a Yeezu soore ba, “Yɛ teɛre ka a Galili noba bama a zubie ŋa naŋ da pɔge da e la yelbe-erebɛ gaŋ a ba Galili taaba zaa be?

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
text: Ai, maa yeli yɛ, ka yɛnee ba leɛ yɛ eebo, yɛ meŋ zaa na wɛ la aŋa ba naŋ da wɛ lɛ.

ref: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
text: A lɛ la ka Yeezu soore, “Wola ka a Naaŋmen naaloŋ waa? Boŋ ka N na de o manne ne?

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
text: Ai, maa yeli yɛ, ka yɛnee ba leɛ yɛ eebo, yɛ meŋ zaa na wɛ la aŋa ba naŋ da wɛ lɛ.” Kaŋkanteɛ Naŋ Ba Wɔnɔ Sekpɔgre

ref: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da lɔɔ sekpɔgre ŋa, “Dɔɔ kaŋ la sɛle kaŋkanteɛ o dãã teere weɛ poɔ, a pãã da wa boɔrɔ wɔmɔ a teɛ zu kyɛ ba nyɛ wɔne zaa.

ref: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
text: Ka a neɛ naŋ kaara a teere zu yeli ko o, ‘Neŋkpoŋ, vɛŋ ka a teɛ naŋ are yuon-yeni, ka N tu gɔlle o, a eŋ kuoluŋ.

ref: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
text: Ka o naŋ wa wɔŋ a yuoni kaŋ, a na soma la, kyɛ ka o naŋ ba wɔŋ, ka fo pãã kyɛ o bare.’ ” Yeezu Naŋ Saŋ Pɔge Gbɛre Kaŋ

ref: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
text: A Yeezu da kanna la noba a puoruudie kaŋ poɔ a pɛnnoo bebiri daare.

ref: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa nyɛ o, O da boɔle o la ka o wa O zie, ka O yeli ko o, “Pɔge, a fo baaloŋ baarɛɛ la.”

ref: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
text: Ka Yeezu yeli sɔge o, “Yɛnee bampuori-kombampoɔ mine! Yɛ buo soba la ba maŋ yuo a o naadɛre bee a o boŋɔ ka o yi nyu koɔ̃ a pɛnnoo bebiri daare?

ref: This woman belongs to the family of Abraham, but Satan has kept her bound for
text: A ba seŋ ka a Aboraham pɔgeyaa ŋa Setaana naŋ leŋ yuomo pie ne anii ŋa zaa na nyɛ o faabo a pɛnnoo bebiri be?”

ref: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa yeli a ŋaa, vi da kpɛ la a noba na zaa naŋ da kyiiri O, kyɛ ka a noba da kyellɛ ne a nɔmaa toma na zaa O naŋ da tonɔ. Bombi-bile Sekpɔgre (Mat

ref: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
text: A gogo na poɔŋ la ka Faraziimine da wa a Yeezu zie a wa yeli ko O, “Yi a kyɛ gaa ziyuo, bonso, Hɛrɔde boɔrɔ la fo koobo.”

ref: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
text: Kyɛ zenɛ ne bieu a ferɛɛ la ka N be N sori poɔ gɛrɛ, bonso, ba kontoɔ̃ ko a Ŋmen-yelmanna zie zaa ka a Gyɛruzɛlɛm poɔŋ naane.’

ref: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
text: Serɛzaa, ba mine be la puoriŋ a na wa de weɛ kyɛ ka ba mine meŋ de weɛ kyɛ na leɛ wa be puoriŋ.” Yeezu Naŋ Koŋ A Gyɛruzɛlɛm Teŋɛ Yɛlɛ (Mat

ref: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
text: Ka a Yeezu yeli ko ba, “Yɛ gaa te yeli ko a badɛre na ko ma, ‘N digre la sefaare bara kyɛ saŋ na baalba zenɛ ane bieu, kyɛ a beri ata daare N na baare la N toma.

ref: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
text: O waa ŋa tobiri ka neɛ de bore o weɛ poɔ. Ka o baa a te leɛ teɛ ka nuuli wa wuo a kyogri o ulli zu.” Dããbile Sekpɔgre (Mat

ref: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
text: A Yeezu da la soorɛɛ la, “Boŋ ka N na de manne ne a Naaŋmen naaloŋ?

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
text: A Yeezu naŋ da gɛrɛ Gyɛruzɛlɛm, O da kpɛ la a teŋkponni ane a tembilii poore a kanna a noba gɛrɛ.

ref: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
text: “Yɛ eŋ faŋ ka yɛ tu a dendɔre na naŋ e fogloo kpɛ; bonso, maa yele korɔ yɛ, noba yaga na e la a neɛ ka ba kpɛ kyɛ kontoɔ̃.

text: “A Naaŋmen begɛ ane a Ŋmen-yelmanneba sɛgre da bebe la te ta a Gyɔɔn Bati waabo saŋ. A pãã yi a lɛ saŋ waana, ba moɔle la a Naaŋmen naaloŋ yelnoore yɛlɛ, ka neɛzaa moɔrɔ ka o fere kpɛ.

ref: Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
text: Kyɛ a na e la mɔlɔ ka ka sazu ne teŋɛ baare a gaŋ a Naaŋmen begɛ yelbi-bile kaŋ naŋ na iri bare.”

ref: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
text: “Dɔɔ zaa naŋ bare o pɔgɔ kyɛ de pɔge yuo, toŋ la moɔrɔ toma. Ka dɔɔ na meŋ naŋ de pɔge na o serɛ naŋ bare meŋ toŋ la moɔrɔ toma.” A Bondaana Ane Lazario Yɛlɛ

ref: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
text: “Bondaana kaŋ la da bebe a maŋ su kpar-veɛle, kyɛ dire bondi veɛle bebiri zaa.

ref: He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them.
text: A Lazario da maŋ wa boɔrɔ la a bondi-purimo naŋ maŋ ŋmaara lere a bondaana diibu zie ka ka o maa ne o kɔŋ. Haali, baare da maŋ wa lɛnnɛ la a o felɛ.

ref: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
text: A nembaala ŋa da kpie la ka malekere da wa de o gaa ne ka o te be Aboraham zie. A bondaana na meŋ da kpie la ka ba ũũ o.

ref: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,

ref: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
text: Lɛ la ka o boɔle, ‘N saa Aboraham, zo N nembaaloŋ kyɛ e ka Lazario de o nubiri tuŋ koɔ̃ poɔ a wa tɔ ne n zele ka o yɛŋ buri belaa lɛ, bonso, N nyɛrɛ la woore yaga a vũũ zele ŋa poɔ.’

ref: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
text: Ka Aboraham da yeli, ‘N bie, leɛ-teɛre nyɛ a fo nyɔvore saŋ, fo da nyɛ la a bonsonne na zaa fo naŋ da maŋ boɔrɔ, kyɛ ka Lazario eŋ da nyɛrɛ woore yoŋ. Pampana ŋa, o dire la noɔ a kyɛ, kyɛ ka fo dire tuo.

ref: But the rich man said, “Abraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home.

text: “A yɛ buo soba la na yeli ko o gbaŋgbaa naŋ yi koɔbo bee peere kyeemo zie wa, ‘Wa kyɛ wieouŋ zaa, a wa zeŋ di diibu’?
