ref: Philip then found Nathanael and said, “We have found the one that Moses and the Prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
text: A Filipi da nyɛ la Nataneɛl a yeli ko o, “Te nyɛ la a neɛ na yɛlɛ a Moozul naŋ sɛge a Naaŋmen begɛ gane poɔ, ka a Ŋmen-yelmanneba meŋ sɛge a gansoŋ poɔ. Ona la a Yeezu, a Yuzɛb bidɔɔ naŋ yi Naazɛrɛ teŋɛ.”

ref: Nathanael asked, “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Philip answered, “Come and see.”
text: A Nataneɛl da soore o la, “Boŋ bonsoŋ la na baŋ yi a Naazɛrɛ poɔ?” Ka Filipi da yeli ko o, “Wa nyɛ.”

ref: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, “Here is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.”
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa nyɛ Nataneɛl ka o waana O zie la ka O da yeli, “Izerayɛl bie sereŋ la a ŋa, ka bonnoo zaa ba kye o zie!”

ref: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”
text: Ka Nataneɛl da soore O, “Wola ka fo e baŋ ma?” Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge o, “N da nyɛ fo la a kaŋkanteɛ pare sɛre kyɛ ka a Filipi pãã wa boɔle fo.”

ref: Nathanael said, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!”
text: Ka Nataneɛl da leɛ yeli, “Wulwullo, fõõ la a Naaŋmen Bie! Fõõ la a Izerayɛl Naa!”

ref: Jesus answered, “Did you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge o, “N naŋ yeli ko fo ka N da nyɛ fo la a kaŋkanteɛ pare la so ka fo sage de be? Fo na nyɛ la yeltegrɛ naŋ gaŋ a ama.”

ref: I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Yelmeŋɛ ka N yele korɔ yɛ. Yɛ na nyɛ la a saluoni ka o yuo, ka a Naaŋmen malekere duoro kyɛ sigre waana a Nensaala Bie zu.”

ref: Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee.
text: A beri ata daare, pɔgde tigri da be la a Keeina teŋɛ naŋ be a Galili paaloŋ poɔ, ka Yeezu ma da be a be.

ref: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
text: Ba da boɔle la Yeezu ane O potuuribo meŋ a pɔgde tigri zie.

ref: When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, “They don't have any more wine.”
text: A tigri dãã naŋ da wa baare, a Yeezu ma da yeli ko O la, “A ba dãã baarɛɛ la.”

ref: Jesus replied, “Mother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Pɔge deme, maa ne fo boŋ yeli la be a poɔ? A N saŋ naŋ ba ta sɛre.”

ref: Mary then said to the servants, “Do whatever Jesus tells you to do.”
text: Ka a O ma da yeli a tontonema, “Yɛ e yelzaa O naŋ na yeli ko yɛ.”

ref: At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about
text: Kyɛ senne ayoɔbo da be la a be a seŋ a Gyuumine puoruu pɛgebo yɛlɛ naŋ da biŋ lɛ. A seŋɛ kanzaa poɔ koɔ̃ da maŋ ta la galori lezare bee lezare ne pie.

ref: Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled,
text: A Yeezu da yeli ko la a noba, “Yɛ ɔŋ koɔ̃ paale a senne.” Ka ba da ɔŋ a koɔ̃ paale a zaa zɛlɛlɛ.

ref: he said, “Now take some water and give it to the man in charge of the feast.” The servants did as Jesus told them,
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Yɛ pãã ɔŋ a mine gaa te ko a noba naŋ kaara a tigri yɛlɛ neŋkpoŋ.” Lɛ la ka ba ɔŋ gaa ne.

ref: and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over
text: A tig-kaara neŋkpoŋ naŋ da wa lɛnne a koɔ̃ naŋ leɛ dãã, kyɛ ba baŋ benee a naŋ yi , da boɔle la a pɔgderɛ

ref: and said, “The best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!”
text: a yeli ko o, “Neɛzaa maŋ daŋ tere la a dãã anaŋ naŋ kpɛ, ka a saŋ na a saama naŋ wa nyu te seŋ, ka o pãã tere a anaŋ naŋ ba kpɛ. Kyɛ ka fõõ eŋ biŋ a dãã anaŋ naŋ kpɛ te ta ŋaa saŋ.”

ref: But Jesus was talking about his body as a temple.
text: Kyɛ a Yeezu da yelee O meŋɛ eŋɛ naŋ waa a Naaŋmen die yɛlɛ.

ref: Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem.
text: A Gyuumine Ista tigri naŋ da wa peɛlɛ, Yeezu da do la Gyɛruzɛlɛm.

ref: So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
text: A Yeezu da de la mie a maale ne kpaaraa a digi ne ba ka ba yi a Naaŋmen die poɔ, a laŋ ne a nii, ane a peere. O da kyiri la a libi-tɛgrebɛ warbie yaare bare kyɛ daa a ba tabolɔ lɔɔ.

ref: The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, “My love for your house burns in me like a fire.”
text: A O potuuribo pãã da leɛ teɛrɛɛ la ka ba da sɛge la a gansoŋ poɔ, “A fo yiri nɔmmo na di ma la aŋa vũũ.”

ref: “Destroy this temple,” Jesus answered, “and in three days I will build it again!”
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge ba, “Yɛ ŋmore a Naaŋmen die ŋa, kyɛ beri ata poɔ N na e la ka o leɛ-iri are.”

ref: There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables.
text: O da nyɛ la noba a Naaŋmen die poɔ ka ba koɔrɔ naadɛɛ, peere ane ŋmama, kyɛ ka a libi-tɛgrebɛ da zeŋ a ba libie tɛgebo zie.

ref: Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, “Get those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.”
text: O da yeli ko la a banaŋ naŋ da koɔrɔ a ŋmama, “Yɛ de a boma ŋa yi te bare! Yɛ ta de a N Saa yiri ka o leɛ yɛ daa diibu zie!”

ref: The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, “What miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?”
text: Lɛ la ka a Gyuumine da soore a Yeezu, “Boŋ yelwulaa la ka fo na e a wuli te ka a seŋ ka fo e a ŋaa.”

ref: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa leɛ-iri a kuuni poɔ puoriŋ la ka a O potuuribo pãã da leɛ-teɛre ka O da yeli la a ŋaa. Ba pãã da sage de la a gansoŋ yɛlɛ ane a Yeezu yɛlɛ O naŋ da yeli.

ref: In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles.
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa be a Gyɛruzɛlɛm poɔ a Ista tigri saŋ, noba yaga da sage de la O yɛlɛ, bonso, ba da nyɛ la a yelwulii O naŋ da erɛ.

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him.
text: Kyɛ Yeezu da koŋ baŋ de O meŋɛ dɛle ba kanzaa, bonso, O da baŋ la nensaalba zaa,

ref: No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.
text: ka O da ba la boɔrɔ neɛzaa ka o yeli neɛ yɛlɛ ko O, bonso O meŋɛ da baŋ la neɛzaa poteɛrɛ.

ref: There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader.
text: A Farazii kaŋ da bebe la ka o yuori di Nikodemu. O da waa la a Gyuumine wederɛ kaŋ.

ref: One night he went to Jesus and said, “Rabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.”
text: O da wa la Yeezu zie tensogɔ saŋ a wa yeli ko O, “Wulwullo, te baŋ ka fo waa la wulwullo naŋ yi Naaŋmen zie. Bonso, neɛzaa koŋ baŋ e a yelwulii ama fo naŋ erɛ ka Naaŋmen ba kye o soba zie.”

ref: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge o, “Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ fo, neɛzaa koŋ baŋ kpɛ a Naaŋmen naaloŋ poɔ, ka ba naŋ ba la maaleŋ dɔge o soba.”

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ fo, neɛzaa koŋ baŋ kpɛ a Naaŋmen naaloŋ ka ba naŋ ba leɛ dɔge o ne koɔ̃ ane a Vooroŋ Soŋ.

ref: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
text: Bon eŋgane naŋ dɔge maŋ waa la eŋgane, ka bon Vooroŋ Soŋ meŋ naŋ dɔge maŋ waa vooroŋ.

ref: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
text: Ta vɛŋ ka fo noɔre maa ne N naŋ yeli ko fo, ‘A ferɛɛ la ka ba leɛ dɔge fo.’

ref: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
text: Saseɛ maŋ fuuro gɛrɛ la benee zaa o naŋ boɔrɔ, ka fo maŋ woŋ a o fuubu dɔɔne, kyɛ ba baŋ benee o naŋ yi, bee benee o naŋ gɛrɛ. Lɛ ka a maŋ waa ko neɛ na zaa a Vooroŋ Soŋ naŋ dɔge.”

ref: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge o, “Fo waa la a Izerayɛl biiri wulwullo kaŋ, kyɛ ba baŋ a yɛlɛ ama?”

ref: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
text: “Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ fo, yeli na te naŋ baŋ la ka te yele, kyɛ dire bonna te naŋ nyɛ danseɛ, kyɛ ka yɛ ba maŋ sage de a te danseɛ.

ref: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
text: Ka maa yeli teŋɛzu yɛlɛ ko fo kyɛ ka fo ba sage de, ka maa pãã yeli tenvelaa yɛlɛ ko fo, wola ka fo pãã na e sage de?”

ref: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
text: Neɛzaa daŋ ba do tenvelaa, ka a soba na naŋ yi a tenvelaa sigi wa yoŋ naane. Ona la a Nensaala Bie.

ref: And the Son of Man must be lifted up, just as the metal snake was lifted up by Moses in the desert.
text: Aŋa lɛ Moozul naŋ da zɛge a kuri waabo ka o do saa a wɛgyɛŋ poɔ, lɛ meŋ la ka a fere ka ba zɛge a Nensaala Bie ka O do saa.

ref: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
text: Ka neɛ na zaa naŋ na sage de O yɛlɛ na nyɛ nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo.

ref: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
text: “Naaŋmen naŋ nɔŋ a tendaa noba yaga la so ka O de O Bidɔɔ yeni tere, ka ka neɛ na zaa naŋ na sage de O yɛlɛ na ta wɛ, kyɛ nyɛ nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo.”

ref: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
text: “Yelmeŋɛ zaa, Naaŋmen ba toŋ a O Bidɔɔ a teŋɛzu ka O wa di noba sɛrɛɛ. Kyɛ O waɛ la ka a tendaa noba na tu O zie nyɛ faabo.

ref: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
text: Banaŋ naŋ sage de O yɛlɛ koŋ le sɛrɛɛ poɔ, kyɛ banaŋ naŋ ba sage de O yɛlɛ le la sɛrɛɛ poɔ baare, ba naŋ ba sage de a Naaŋmen Bi-yeni yuori zuiŋ.

ref: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
text: Kyɛ a sɛrɛɛ na poɔ noba naŋ na le la ŋa: veɛloŋ wa la a teŋɛzu, kyɛ ka noba nɔŋ ligruŋ gaŋ veɛloŋ, ba yel-erre naŋ da waa faa zuiŋ.

ref: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
text: A lɛ zuiŋ, banaŋ zaa naŋ erɛ yelfaare kyiiri la a veɛloŋ, a koŋ sage kpɛ a veɛloŋ poɔ, ka a ba yelfaare na ta sãã.

ref: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
text: Kyɛ banaŋ naŋ tuuro a yelmeŋɛ sori maŋ yire la a veɛloŋ poɔ, ka ba na nyɛ ka a sãã weliweli ka a ba yel-erre yi la Naaŋmen zie.” A Yeezu Ane Gyɔɔn Bati Yɛlɛ

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
text: A ŋaa puoriŋ, Yeezu ane O potuuribo da gaa la Gyudiya paaloŋ, ka O ne ba da te be a be ka a kɔɔre belaa kyɛ da korɔ noba suobu.

ref: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
text: A Gyɔɔn potuuribo ane a Gyuu neɛ kaŋ da ŋmeɛrɛ la nɔkpeɛne a kyaare ne dɛgre peeroo yɛlɛ.

ref: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
text: A Gyɔɔn potuuribo da wa la o zie a da wa yeli ko o, “Wulwullo, a neɛ na naŋ da wa fo zie a Gyɔɔdan baa gaŋne ka fo da yeli o yɛlɛ, korɔ la noba suobu, ka a noba zaa zoro gɛrɛ a o zie.”

ref: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
text: Ka a Gyɔɔn da yeli sɔge, “Neɛzaa koŋ baŋ taa bonzaa, ka bon Naaŋmen naŋ de ko o soba naane.”

ref: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
text: Yɛ menne zaa tɔre di la danseɛ ne N naŋ da yeli, ‘Maa naane a Mɛzaaya, kyɛ ba toŋ ma la ka N de O weɛ wa.’

ref: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
text: Neɛ naŋ so a pɔgpaalaa la a o serɛ. A dɔɔ-zeŋguuro naŋ maŋ are guuro a serpaalaa poɔ maŋ pɛle la yaga ka o naŋ wa woŋ a serpaalaa kɔkɔre. A ŋaa zuiŋ la ka N poɔ pɛle yaga.

ref: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
text: A ferɛɛ la ka O yuori duoro kyɛ ka maa deni sigre. Soba Na Naŋ Yi Tenvelaa Wa Yɛlɛ

ref: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
text: A soba na naŋ yi sazu wa, gaŋ la neɛzaa. Soba na meŋ naŋ yi a teŋɛzu e la a teŋɛzu neɛ, a maŋ yele teŋɛzu yɛlɛ. A soba na naŋ yi tenvelaa wa gaŋ la neɛzaa.

ref: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
text: O di la danseɛ kyaare ne bon O naŋ nyɛ kyɛ woŋ, kyɛ neɛzaa ba sage de O danseɛ diibu.

ref: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
text: Soba na zaa naŋ sage de a O danseɛ, sage deɛ la ka, Naaŋmen waa la yelmeŋɛ soba.

ref: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
text: A soba na Naaŋmen naŋ toŋ, maŋ yelee Naaŋmen yelbie, bonso, Naaŋmen maŋ ko o la O Vooroŋ Soŋ ka a ba taa mannoo.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
text: A Saa nɔŋ la a Bidɔɔ, a de boma zaa eŋ O nuuriŋ.

ref: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
text: Soba na zaa naŋ sage de a Bidɔɔ yɛlɛ, taa la nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo. Soba na zaa meŋ naŋ zagre a Bidɔɔ yɛlɛ koŋ nyɛ nyɔvore, kyɛ ka Naaŋmen supuriyaaraa dɔgle o zu.

ref: Jesus left Judea and started for Galilee again.
text: O da yi la a Gyudiya paaloŋ a leɛ gaa a Galili paaloŋ.

ref: This time he had to go through Samaria,
text: Kyɛ a da ferɛɛ la ka O tu a Samareya paaloŋ poɔ gaa a be.

ref: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
text: A Samareya pɔge da soore la a Yeezu, “Wola ka fõõ Gyuu neɛ na sɔre koɔ̃ maa naŋ e Samareya neɛ zie?”

ref: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
text: Ka a pɔge da yeli ko O, “Daana, fo ba taa bon na ɔŋ ne a koɔ̃, kyɛ ka a bulee waa zuluŋ; yeŋ ka fo na nyɛ a kɔŋvore?

ref: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
text: Fo mɔɔ waa la kpoŋ gaŋ a te sããkoma Gyekɔb naŋ da ko te a bulee ŋa, ka o biiri ne ba donne zaa da nyuuro a bulee ŋa koɔ̃ be?”

ref: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
text: Kyɛ banaŋ naŋ nyu a koɔ̃ N naŋ na ko ba, kɔŋnyuuri daŋ koŋ la kpɛ ba zaa. A koɔ̃ na N naŋ na tere, na leɛ la bulnimiri ba poɔŋ, ka a bullo yire a terɛ nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo.”

ref: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
text: Ka a pɔge da yeli ko O, “Daana, ko ma a koɔ̃ ŋa, ka kɔŋnyuuri ta la kpɛ ma zaa, bee ka N ta la waana kyɛ ɔnnɔ koɔ̃.”

ref: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Gaa te boɔle fo serɛ, kyɛ leɛ wa.”

ref: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
text: bonso, serebɛ banuu la ka fo kuli, kyɛ a onaŋ fo naŋ taa pampana ŋa ba waa fo serɛ. A lɛ fo naŋ yeli e la yelmeŋɛ!”

ref: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
text: Ka a pɔge da yeli ko O, “Daana, N nyɛɛ la ka fo waa la Ŋmen-yelmanna.

ref: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
text: Te sããkommine da maŋ puori la Naaŋmen a taŋ ŋa zu, kyɛ ka yɛnee Gyuumine yeli ka zie na a naŋ fere ka noba maŋ puori Naaŋmen la Gyɛruzɛlɛm teŋɛ poɔ.”

ref: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Pɔge, sage de N yɛlɛ. A saŋ waana la ka yɛ koŋ la puoro a Saa a taŋ ŋa zu bee Gyɛruzɛlɛm poɔ.

ref: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
text: Yɛ Samareya noba puoro la bon yɛ naŋ ba baŋ, kyɛ tenee Gyuumine puoro la bon te naŋ baŋ, bonso, a Gyuumine zie la ka faabo yi.

ref: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
text: Kyɛ a saŋ tara la, a saŋ gba taɛ la, ka banaŋ naŋ puoro ne ba poore zaa na puori a Saa ne a vooroŋ ane yelmennoŋ. Bonso, a bama taaba la ka a Saa boɔrɔ ka ba puori O.

ref: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
text: Naaŋmen waa la vooroŋ, ka banaŋ naŋ puoro O, seŋ ka ba puori O a vooroŋ ane yelmennoŋ poɔ.”

ref: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
text: Ka a pɔge da yeli ko O, “N baŋ ka a Mɛzaaya waana la” . “Ka Onaŋ wa wa, O na manne la yelzaa ko te.”

ref: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
text: Ka Yeezu yeli ko o, “Maa la a lɛ, a soba ŋa naŋ yele a yɛlɛ korɔ fo.”

ref: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
text: A lɛ poɔŋ meŋ la ka O potuuribo da leɛ yi a teŋɛ poɔ wa. Ba nɔɛ zaa da maaɛ la ne ba naŋ wa nyɛ O ka O ne pɔge yele yɛlɛ. Kyɛ neɛzaa da ba soore, “Boŋ ka fo boɔrɔ?” bee, “Bonso ka fo yele yɛlɛ ne a pɔge?”

ref: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
text: Lɛ la ka a pɔge da bare a o koɔ̃ yuori kyɛ da leɛ gaa a teŋkpoŋ poɔ a te yeli ko a noba:

ref: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
text: “Yɛ wa nyɛ dɔɔ kaŋ naŋ yeli n nyɔvore yɛlɛ zaa N naŋ da e a ko ma! Ona mɔɔ tɔɔ wa la a Mɛzaaya wɛ?”

ref: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
text: A noba da yi la a teŋɛ poɔ a gaa a Yeezu zie.

ref: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
text: Kyɛ a O potuuribo da sɔre O la a yele, “Daana, di bonkaŋ wɛ.”

ref: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
text: Kyɛ ka O da yeli ko ba, “N taa la bondirii na di kyɛ ka yɛ naŋ ba baŋ a yɛlɛ.”

ref: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
text: Lɛ la ka a potuuribo da leɛ soorɔ taa, “Neɛ kaŋ tɔɔ wa taa bondirii wa ko O wɛ?”

ref: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “N bondirii la ka N toŋ a soba na naŋ toŋ ma poboɔbo; a toŋ ka a te baare.

ref: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
text: Yɛ mɔɔ ba maŋ yeli, ‘Kyuuri anaare la kyɛre ka a weɛ boma deebo saŋ na ta’? Kyɛ maa yele korɔ yɛ, yɛ leɛ kaa a weɛ gyeere, a nyɛ lɛ a weɛ boma naŋ be seŋ deebo.

ref: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
text: A weɛ bonkyeɛrɛ nyɛrɛ la o toma san-yɔɔ, a pãã wuoro tewɔmɔ laŋna taa a nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo eŋɛ, ka a boreborɔ ane a weɛ bonkyeɛrɛ na lantaa kyellɛ.

ref: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
text: Lɛ la ka a yeluu ŋa e yelmeŋɛ, ‘Neɛ kaŋ maŋ bore la, kyɛ ka kaŋ meŋ kyɛ.’

ref: I am sending you to harvest crops in fields where others have done all the hard work.
text: N toŋ yɛ la ka yɛ te kyɛ boma na yɛ naŋ ba toŋ ka a yi. Noba mine la toŋ ka a yi, ka yɛ te kpɛ a ba toma poɔ.”

ref: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
text: A Samareya noba yaga naŋ da yi a teŋkpoŋ na poɔ da sage de la a Yeezu yɛlɛ a yi a danseɛ a pɔge naŋ da di zuiŋ, “O da yeli la yɛlɛ na zaa N naŋ da e n nyɔvore poɔ ko ma.”

ref: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
text: Azuiŋ, a Samareya noba naŋ da wa a Yeezu zie, ba da sɔre O la ka O kpeɛrɛ ne ba. A Yeezu da kpɛ la a be beri ayi.

ref: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
text: Ka noba yaga da sage de O yɛlɛ a O kannoo zuiŋ.

ref: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
text: Ba da yeli ko la a pɔge, “A ba la e ka fo yeluu la so ka te sage de O yɛlɛ, bonso, temenne woŋ la a O kannoo a baŋ ka serɛzaa, Ona la a andonɛɛ Faara.” Yeezu Naŋ Leɛ Gaa A Galili Paaloŋ

ref: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
text: O naŋ da wa a Galili, a teŋɛ deme da tuori de O la, ba naŋ da nyɛ bonna zaa O naŋ da e a tigri zie a Gyɛruzɛlɛm poɔ, bonso, ba meŋ da gaa la a tigri zie. Yeezu Naŋ Da Saŋ Neŋkpoŋ Kaŋ Bidɔɔ

ref: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da leɛ gaa a Keeina teŋɛ naŋ be Galili paaloŋ poɔ, a be O naŋ da e ka koɔ̃ leɛ dãã. A naayiri tonton-neŋkpoŋ kaŋ da be la a be, ka o bidɔɔ beɛrɛ gaŋ Kapanum teŋɛ poɔ.

ref: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
text: O naŋ da wa woŋ ka Yeezu yi la a Gyudiya paaloŋ wa Galili, o da gaa te sɔre O la, ka O wa saŋ a o bidɔɔ, bonso a bie da tɔgle la kũũ.

ref: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Ka yɛnee ba nyɛ yelwulii ane nɔmaa toma, yɛ koŋ sage de.”

ref: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
text: Ka a tonton-neŋkpoŋ da yeli ko O, “Neŋkpoŋ, naŋ sigi wa ka a n bie ta wa kpi.”

ref: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Gɛrɛ, a fo bidɔɔ na nyɛ la laafeɛ.” A dɔɔ da sage de la yɛlɛ a Yeezu naŋ da yeli ko o, a da leɛ kulo.

ref: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
text: O naŋ da naŋ be a sobiriŋ kyɛnɛ kulo la ka a o gbaŋgbaare zo tuori o a da yeli ko o ka a o bie nyɛ la laafeɛ.

ref: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
text: Lɛ la ka o da soore ba wagre na poɔ naŋ da la ka a bie da piili nyɛrɛ o meŋɛ. Ka ba da yeli ko O, “Zããmeŋ ŋmenaa gbel-yeni saŋ la ka a o entoloŋ da baare.”

ref: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
text: A bie saa da baŋɛɛ la ka a saŋ ŋa poɔ la ka a Yeezu da yeli ko o, “A fo bidɔɔ na nyɛ la laafeɛ.” Lɛ la ka o ne o yideme zaa da sage de a Yeezu yɛlɛ.

ref: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
text: A ŋa da la a yelwulaa ayi soba Yeezu naŋ da e, O naŋ da yi a Gyudiya leɛ gaa a Galili paaloŋ.

ref: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
text: A yɛlɛ ama puoriŋ a Gyuumine tigri kaŋ da taɛ la, ka Yeezu da do gaa Gyɛruzɛlɛm.

ref: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
text: Koɔ̃-puu zie kaŋ da be la a Gyɛruzɛlɛm poɔ a peɛle a dendɔre na ba naŋ boɔlɔ Peere Dendɔre ka ba boɔlɔ o Bɛsaada a Hiburu deme kɔkɔre poɔ. A be da taa la kyaare anuu.

ref: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
text: Dɔɔ kaŋ naŋ da le baaloŋ yuomo lezare ne pie ne anii da gaŋ la a be.

ref: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
text: Yɛ ba taa O yelbiri ka o be yɛ nyɔvore poɔ, yɛ naŋ ba sage de a soba na O naŋ toŋ yɛlɛ zuiŋ.”

ref: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa nyɛ a dɔɔ baala ka o gaŋ a be, kyɛ ka O da baŋ ka o gaŋ la a be ka a kɔɔre, O da soore la a dɔɔ, “Fo boɔrɔ la ka fo nyɛ laafeɛ be?”

ref: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
text: Ka a dɔɔ baala da yeli sɔge O, “Neŋkpoŋ, N ba taa neɛzaa naŋ na nyɔge ma eŋ a koɔ̃ poɔ saŋ na ba naŋ maŋ wa dɔ̃ɔ̃ a koɔ̃. N naŋ maŋ wa gaa ka N sigre kpeɛrɛ zaa ka n tɔ kaŋ daŋ ma la kpɛ.”

ref: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Vaa-iri de a fo boŋganaa kyɛnɛ gɛrɛ.”

ref: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
text: A daadaalɛ ka a dɔɔ deɛ nyɛ laafeɛ, a de o boŋganaa a da piili kyɛnɛ. A bebiri ŋa da waa la a Gyuumine pɛnnoo bebiri daare.

ref: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
text: Lɛ la ka a Gyuumine da yeli ko a dɔɔ naŋ da nyɛ o sammo, “Zenɛ waa la a pɛnnoo bebiri, sori kyebe ka fo tuo fo boŋganaa.”

ref: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
text: Kyɛ ka o da yeli sɔge ba, “A dɔɔ na naŋ saŋ ma la yeli ko ma, ‘De a fo boŋganaa kyɛnɛ gɛrɛ.’ ”

ref: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
text: Ka ba da soore o, “Aŋ la a dɔɔ ŋa naŋ yeli ko fo, ‘De a fo boŋganaa kyɛnɛ gɛrɛ’?”

ref: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
text: A dɔɔ da ba baŋ a soba na naŋ saŋ o, bonso, a Yeezu da kpɛ la a nembeɛoŋ poɔ a tɔle te gaa.

ref: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
text: A lɛ puoriŋ, a Yeezu da te nyɛ o la a Naaŋmen die poɔ a da yeli ko o, “Nyɛ, fo nyɛ la laafeɛ! Tɔɔ daŋ la wa erɛ yelbieri zaa ka yelfaa zaa ta wa pɔge fo.”

ref: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
text: A dɔɔ da yi gaa te yeli ko la a Gyuumine ka a Yeezu la da saŋ o.

ref: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
text: A lɛ zuiŋ, a Gyuumine da piili korɔ la a Yeezu dɔgroŋ, O naŋ da erɛ a yɛlɛ ama taaba a pɛnnoo bebiri daare zuiŋ.

ref: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
text: Kyɛ ka a Yeezu da leɛ yeli sɔge ba, “A N Saa naŋ kyɛnɛ tonɔ la toma, ka N meŋ naŋ tonɔ.”

ref: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
text: A yeli ŋa zuiŋ, a Gyuumine da maaleŋ moɔ la nimiri a boɔrɔ ka ba ko O. Bonso, a pɛnnoo bebiri kyiiruŋ yoŋ naane ka O da saana, kyɛ O naŋ da boɔlɔ Naaŋmen O Saa, ka a lɛ na wuli ka O de la O meŋɛ ka O ne Naaŋmen seŋ taa. A Bidɔɔ Naŋ Taa Kpeɛ̃õ Yɛlɛ

ref: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, a Bidɔɔ koŋ baŋ e yelzaa ne O meŋɛ faŋ, ka a naŋ ba e yeli na O naŋ nyɛ a Saa ka O erɛ yoŋ naane. Bonso, yeli na zaa a Saa naŋ erɛ, a lɛ ne a zu la ka a Bidɔɔ meŋ maŋ e.

ref: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
text: A Saa nɔŋ la a Bidɔɔ, a maŋ wuli O a anaŋ zaa Ona meŋɛ naŋ maŋ erɛ. A Saa na wuli la a Bidɔɔ yelbɛrɛ naŋ gaŋ ŋaa, ka a na e yɛ nɔmaa.

ref: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
text: Serɛzaa, aŋa a Saa naŋ maŋ seŋ a kuuni lɛ, a e ka ba leɛ voorɔ, a lɛ meŋ ka a Bidɔɔ meŋ maŋ ko banaŋ zaa O naŋ boɔrɔ, nyɔvore.

ref: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
text: A Saa ba dire neɛzaa sɛrɛɛ, kyɛ O de la sɛrɛɛdiibu zaa ko a Bidɔɔ,

ref: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
text: ka ka noba zaa na de emmo ko a Bidɔɔ aŋa lɛ ba naŋ eŋnɛ a Saa. Soba na zaa naŋ ba eŋnɛ a Bidɔɔ, ba eŋnɛ a Saa naŋ toŋ a Bidɔɔ.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
text: Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, soba na zaa naŋ woŋ N yelbie, a sage de a soba na naŋ toŋ ma yɛlɛ, taa la nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo. Ba koŋ di o soba sɛrɛɛ, kyɛ ka o yi kuuni poɔ a te kpɛ nyɔvore poɔ.”

ref: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
text: “Serɛzaa ka N yeli ko yɛ, a saŋ tara la; a saŋ gba taɛ la pampana ŋa, ka kuuni na woŋ a Naaŋmen Bie kɔkɔre. Kyɛ banaŋ naŋ na woŋ a kɔkɔre na taa la nyɔvore.

ref: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
text: Aŋa a Saa naŋ e a nyɔvore zaa yiibu zie, lɛ la ka O e ka a O Bidɔɔ meŋ e a nyɔvore zaa yiibu zie.

ref: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
text: Kyɛ a Saa ko la a Bidɔɔ kpeɛ̃õ ka O di sɛrɛɛ, O naŋ waa a Nensaala Bie zuiŋ.

ref: Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man,
text: Yɛ ta vɛŋ ka yɛ nɔɛ maa ne a ŋaa, bonso, a saŋ waana la ka banaŋ zaa naŋ be ba yaare poore na woŋ a Naaŋmen Bie kɔkɔre

ref: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
text: a wa yi ba yaare poore. Banaŋ naŋ da erɛ soŋ na iri voorɔ la, kyɛ ka banaŋ naŋ da erɛ faa na iri nyɛ dɔgroŋ.” A Yeezu Dansediribe Yɛlɛ

ref: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
text: “N koŋ baŋ e yelzaa ne N meŋɛ kpeɛ̃õ. A lɛ Naaŋmen naŋ maŋ wuli ma, lɛ ka N maŋ di a sɛrɛɛ kyaare ne. N sɛrɛɛdiibu maŋ e meŋɛ, bonso, N meŋɛ poboɔbo naane ka N erɛ, kyɛ a soba na naŋ toŋ ma poboɔbo la.”

ref: If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth.
text: “Ka maa dire N meŋɛ danseɛ, a N danseɛ ba taa yelmeŋɛ.

ref: But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true.
text: Kyɛ neɛ kaŋ bebe la a dire a N danseɛ. Ka N baŋ ka a O danseɛ O naŋ dire kyaare ne ma waa la yelmeŋɛ.

ref: You sent messengers to John, and he told them the truth.
text: Yɛ da toŋ la yɛ noba a Gyɔɔn zie, ka o da di a yelmeŋɛ danseɛ.

ref: I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
text: A ba e ŋa N boɔrɛɛ nensaala ka o di N danseɛ, kyɛ N yele la a yɛlɛ ama a yɛ faabo zuiŋ.

ref: John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
text: A Gyɔɔn da waa la fentelɛ naŋ dire, a kyaana, ka yɛ da boɔrɔ ka yɛ kyelle belaa a o kyaane poɔ.

ref: But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
text: Kyɛ N taa la danseɛ naŋ gaŋ a Gyɔɔn deni. A toma na N Saa naŋ ko ma ka N na toŋ te baare, a toma na ne a zu N naŋ tonɔ la dire danseɛ ka a Saa la toŋ ma.

ref: The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face.
text: Kyɛ a Saa naŋ toŋ ma, O meŋɛ meŋ di la N danseɛ. Yɛ daŋ ba woŋ O kɔkɔre, bee a nyɛ O waaloŋ.

ref: You search the Scriptures, because you think you will find eternal life in them. The Scriptures tell about me,
text: “Yɛ maŋ sɔre la, a boɔrɔ yɛlɛ a gansoŋ poɔ, bonso, yɛ teɛre ka a poɔ la ka yɛ na nyɛ nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo, kyɛ a gansoŋ ŋa poɔ yɛlɛ la di N danseɛ.

ref: but you refuse to come to me for eternal life.
text: Kyɛ ka yɛ zagre ka yɛ wa N zie a wa nyɛ nyɔvore.

ref: I don't care about human praise,
text: N ba boɔrɔ puubu yi nensaalba zie.

ref: but I do know that none of you love God.
text: Kyɛ N baŋ ka yɛ ba taa Naaŋmen nɔmmo yɛ sukyie poore.

ref: I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own.
text: N wa la N Saa yuori eŋɛ, kyɛ ka yɛ ba sage de ma. Kyɛ ka neɛ kaŋ da wa o meŋɛ yuori eŋɛ, yɛ na sage de o la.

ref: How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
text: Wola ka yɛ na e de Naaŋmen yɛlɛ ka yɛnee sagra derɛ puubu naŋ yire yɛ nensaaltaaba zie, kyɛ ba boɔrɔ puubu naŋ yi a soba na yoŋ naŋ waa Naaŋmen?

ref: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.
text: Yɛ ta teɛre ka N na yeli la yɛ yeli saanaa ko a N Saa. A Moozul yɛ naŋ de yɛ teɛretɔroo eŋ, ona la na ko yɛ saanaa.

ref: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
text: Ka yɛnee da sage de a Moozul yɛlɛ, yɛ da na sage de la N meŋ yɛlɛ, bonso, N yɛlɛ la ka Moozul da sɛge.

ref: But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
text: Kyɛ ka yɛnee ba sage de a yɛlɛ o naŋ sɛge, wola ka yɛ na e sage de N yɛlɛ?”

ref: The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
text: Ba pãã da wuo la a bon ŋmaara a noba naŋ da di kyɛ kyɛre ka a paale pɛre pie ne ayi.

ref: The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed. The Bread That Gives Life
text: Lɛ la ka ba da boɔrɔ ka ba de O eŋ a gbori poɔ, kyɛ ka a gbori deɛ ta a mane noɔre, a lambori na ba naŋ da gɛrɛ. A Bondirii Naŋ Yi Tenvelaa

ref: They replied, “What miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do?
text: Ka ba pãã da soore O, “Boŋ yelwulaa la ka fo na e ka te nyɛ, kyɛ pãã sage de fo yɛlɛ? Boŋ toma la ka fo tonɔ?

ref: No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: Neɛzaa koŋ baŋ wa N zie, ka a Saa naŋ toŋ ma ba tage o soba wa ne, ka N pãã e ka o soba leɛ-iri a bebi-baaraa daare.

ref: “There is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?”
text: “Bidɔɔlee kaŋ be la a kyɛ a taa boroboro anuu ane zoma ayi, kyɛ a ama pãã la boŋ ko a neŋyaga bama?”

ref: The ground was covered with grass, and Jesus told his disciples to tell everyone to sit down. About
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli, “Yɛ e ka a noba zeŋ teŋɛ.” Moɔ yaga da be la a zie, ka a noba da zeŋ teŋɛ. A noba zaa da ta ŋa turi anuu.

ref: They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them,
text: a te kpɛ gbori a piili ka ba ŋmaa a mane gaa Kapanum. Zie da ligee la kyɛ ka Yeezu da naŋ ba wa ba zie sɛre.

ref: Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias.
text: A ŋaa puoriŋ, Yeezu da gaŋ gaa la a Galili Mane lambori kaŋ meŋ ba naŋ da boɔlɔ Tiberiya Mane.

ref: A large crowd had seen him work miracles to heal the sick, and those people went with him.
text: Noba yaga da tuuro O la, ba naŋ da nyɛ a yelwulii O naŋ erɛ korɔ a baalba zuiŋ.

ref: Philip answered, “Don't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?”
text: Ka Filipi da yeli sɔge O, “Kyuuri ayoɔbo san-yɔɔ koŋ baŋ da bondirii ka ba yeni yeni zaa na nyɛ belaa di.”

ref: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said,
text: A O potuuro kaŋ yuori naŋ di Anduru, a Saamon Piita yɔɔ, da yeli ko la a Yeezu:

ref: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da de a boroboro a puori Naaŋmen bareka, kyɛ de poŋ a noba naŋ da zeŋ. A lɛ ne o zu la ka O da de a zommo meŋ e. Ka a noba kanzaa da di ka a seŋ o.

ref: The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted.
text: Ba naŋ da wa di tege baare, Yeezu da yeli ko la a O potuuribo, “Yɛ wuo a bon ŋmaara a noba naŋ di kyɛ kyɛre lantaa ka a ta wɛ.”

ref: Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa baŋ ka a noba da boɔrɔ la ka ba wa fere nyɔge O a e ka O leɛ a ba naa, O da yi la ba poɔ a la te do a taŋ zu a te bebe O yoŋ. Yeezu Naŋ Kyɛŋ Koɔ̃ Zu (Mat

ref: The people who had stayed on the east side of the lake knew that only one boat had been there. They also knew that Jesus had not left in it with his disciples. But the next day
text: A nyaazie daare, a nembeɛoŋ na naŋ da be a mane lambori kaŋ na, da nyɛɛ la ka gbori yeni yoŋ la da be a be. Ba da nyɛɛ la meŋ ka Yeezu da ba kpɛ a gbori ne a O potuuribo, kyɛ a potuuribo yoŋ la da gaa kyɛ bare O.

ref: Don't work for food that spoils. Work for food that gives eternal life. The Son of Man will give you this food, because God the Father has given him the right to do so.”
text: Yɛ ta tonɔ boɔrɔ bondirii naŋ na maŋ wɛ, kyɛ yɛ boɔrɔ anaŋ naŋ na terɛ nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo. Ana la ka a Nensaala Bie na ko yɛ, bonso, Ona la ka a Saa Naaŋmen tere kpeɛ̃õ.”

ref: One of the prophets wrote, “God will teach all of them.” And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
text: Ba da sɛge la a Ŋmen-yelmanneba gane poɔ, ‘Naaŋmen na wuli la ba zaa.’ Neɛzaa naŋ woŋ kyɛ zanne bon naŋ yi a Saa zie maŋ wa la N zie.

ref: They started arguing with each other and asked, “How can he give us his flesh to eat?”
text: A Gyuumine pãã da ŋmeɛrɛ la nɔkpeɛne ne taa a yele, “Wola ka a dɔɔ ŋa na baŋ de O eŋɛ ko te ka te di?”

ref: That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake.
text: Zie naŋ da wa maa, a O potuuribo da sigi la a mane noɔre,

ref: and a strong wind was making the water rough.
text: A mane da zɛlɛɛ la, bonso, sasekpeɛŋaa da fuuro la.

ref: When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified.
text: Ba naŋ da mɔŋ a gbori te ta ŋa mɛlɛ ata bee anaare, ba da nyɛ la a Yeezu ka O kyɛnɛ a koɔ̃ zu a waana a ba gbori zie ka dabeɛ̃ da kpɛ ba yaga.

ref: But he said, “I am Jesus! Don't be afraid!”
text: Kyɛ ka Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Maa la, yɛ ta zoro dabeɛ̃.”

ref: some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks.
text: Lɛ la ka gboe mine da yi Tiberiya a wa are peɛle a zie na a Daana naŋ da de a boroboro a puori Naaŋmen bareka, kyɛ de ko ba ka ba di.

ref: They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
text: Azuiŋ a noba naŋ da nyɛ ka a Yeezu bee a O potuuribo kanzaa da ba la kye a be, ba da kpɛ la a gboe a gaŋ gaa Kapanum a te yɔ boɔrɔ a Yeezu.

ref: They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
text: Ba naŋ da wa nyɛ O a mane lambori na, ba da soore O la, “Wulwullo, boŋ saŋ la ka fo wa a kyɛ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge ba, “Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, yɛ naŋ di a bondirii tege la so ka yɛ boɔrɔ ma, a ba e ŋa yɛ naŋ nyɛ a yelwulii N naŋ e la so.

ref: “What exactly does God want us to do?” the people asked.
text: Lɛ la ka a noba da soore O, “Boŋ ka te na e a toɔ̃ toŋ a Naaŋmen toma?”

ref: Jesus answered, “God wants you to have faith in the one he sent.”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge ba, “A Naaŋmen toma la ŋa: ka yɛ sage de a neɛ na O naŋ toŋ yɛ zie yɛlɛ.”

ref: For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‘God gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
text: A te sããkommine da di la a maana a wɛgyɛŋ poɔ, aŋa lɛ ba naŋ da sɛge a gansoŋ poɔ, ‘O da ko ba la bondirii naŋ yi tenvelaa ka ba di.’ ”

ref: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, Moozul naane da ko yɛ a bondirii na naŋ da yi tenvelaa, kyɛ a N Saa la maŋ tere bondi serɛ naŋ yi tenvelaa.”

ref: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”
text: Bonso, a Naaŋmen bondirii la anaŋ naŋ yi tenvelaa, a maŋ korɔ a andonɛɛ nyɔvore.

ref: The people said, “Sir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!”
text: Ka ba da yeli ko O, “Neŋkpoŋ, korɔ te a bondirii ŋa tegitegilɛ.”

ref: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Maa la a nyɔvore bondiraa. Neɛ na zaa naŋ wa N zie, kɔŋ daŋ koŋ kpɛ o zaa, ka neɛ na zaa naŋ sage de N yɛlɛ, kɔŋnyuuri daŋ koŋ kpɛ o zaa.

ref: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
text: Kyɛ N da yeli ko yɛ la ka yɛ da nyɛ ma la kyɛ da ba sage de N yɛlɛ.

ref: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
text: Bonna zaa N Saa naŋ ko ma na wa la N zie, ka soba na zaa naŋ wa N zie, N koŋ digi o bare,

ref: I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me,
text: bonso, N yi la tenvelaa wa ka N toŋ a soba na naŋ toŋ ma poboɔbo, kyɛ a ba e N meŋɛ poboɔbo.

ref: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
text: Kyɛ a ŋa la a soba na naŋ toŋ ma poboɔbo; ka N ta e ka a banaŋ kanzaa O naŋ ko ma bɔre, kyɛ na e ka ba leɛ-iri a bebi-baaraa daare.

ref: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
text: A N Saa poboɔbo sereŋ la ŋa: ka ka banaŋ zaa naŋ nyɛ a Bidɔɔ, a sage de O yɛlɛ na nyɛ nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaraa, kyɛ ka N e ka ba leɛ-iri a bebi-baaraa daare.”

ref: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
text: Lɛ la ka a Gyuumine da piili zɔɔrɔ yɛlɛ ne a Yeezu, bonso, O naŋ da yeli, “Maa la a bondiraa na naŋ yi tenvelaa wa.”

ref: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
text: A Gyuumine da yelee la, “A Yuzɛb bidɔɔ Yeezu mɔɔ naane a ŋa be? Te baŋ la O saa ne O ma. Wola ka O na baŋ yeli, ‘N yi la tenvelaa sigi wa’?”

ref: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
text: A ba e ŋa neɛzaa la nyɛ a Saa, ka a soba na naŋ yi Naaŋmen zie wa yoŋ naane, ona la nyɛ a Saa.

ref: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
text: Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, neɛ na zaa naŋ wa sage de N yɛlɛ nyɛ la nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaraa.

ref: I am the bread that gives life!
text: Maa la a nyɔvore bondiraa.

ref: Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died.
text: A yɛ sããkommine da di la maana a wɛgyɛŋ poɔ, kyɛ ba da kpie la.

ref: But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die.
text: A bondiraa naŋ yi tenvelaa sigi wa la a ŋa, ka ka neɛ na di a ta kpi.

ref: I am that bread from heaven! Everyone who eats it will live forever. My flesh is the life-giving bread I give to the people of this world.
text: Maa la a nyɔvore bondiraa naŋ yi tenvelaa sigi wa. Neɛ na zaa naŋ di a bondiraa ŋa, na voorɔ la tegitegilɛ. Kyɛ a bondiraa na N naŋ na tere, ka o ko a teŋɛzu deme nyɔvore la a N meŋɛ eŋɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, ka yɛnee ba di a Nensaala Bie eŋɛ a kyɛ nyu O zẽẽ, yɛ koŋ taa nyɔvore.

ref: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
text: Banaŋ naŋ di N eŋɛ kyɛ nyu N zẽẽ taa la nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaraa, kyɛ N na e la ka ba leɛ-iri a bebi-baaraa daare.

ref: My flesh is the true food, and my blood is the true drink.
text: Bonso, N eŋɛ waa la bondiraa meŋɛ, ka N zẽẽ waa bonnyuuraa meŋɛ.

ref: If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: Banaŋ naŋ maŋ di N eŋɛ kyɛ nyu N zẽẽ maŋ be la N poɔ kyɛ ka N meŋ be ba poɔ.

ref: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
text: Aŋa lɛ a Saa vore naŋ toŋ ma, ka N voorɔ a Saa zuiŋ, lɛ meŋ la ka soba na zaa naŋ di N eŋɛ na voorɔ a N zuiŋ.

ref: The bread that comes down from heaven isn't like what your ancestors ate. They died, but whoever eats this bread will live forever.
text: A bondiraa na naŋ da yi a tenvelaa sigi wa la ŋa. O ba waa seŋ ŋa a bondiraa na a yɛ sããkommine naŋ da di a kyɛ naŋ kpi. Kyɛ soba na naŋ di a bondiraa ŋa na voorɔ la tegitegilɛ.”

ref: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
text: O da yeli la a yɛlɛ ama O naŋ da naŋ kanna a puoruudie poɔ a Kapanum teŋɛ poɔ. Nyɔvore Naŋ Ba Taa Baaroo Yɛlɛ

ref: Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, “This is too hard for anyone to understand.”
text: A Yeezu potuuribo yaga naŋ da woŋ a O yelbiri ŋa da yelee la, “A O kannoo ŋa waa la kpeɛŋaa; aŋ la na baŋ sage de a?”

ref: Jesus knew that his disciples were grumbling. So he asked, “Does this bother you?
text: Kyɛ Yeezu naŋ da baŋ ka O potuuribo da ŋmoonɛɛ yɛlɛ, O da soore ba la, “A yeli ŋa sãã la yɛ poɔ be?

ref: What if you should see the Son of Man go up to heaven where he came from?
text: Ka yɛnee pãã da nyɛ a Nensaala Bie ka O leɛ duoro a be O naŋ da be, wola ka yɛ na yeli?

ref: The Spirit is the one who gives life! Human strength can do nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are from that life-giving Spirit.
text: A vooroŋ la maŋ tere nyɔvore; a eŋgane ba taa tɛgɛ. A yelbie na N naŋ da yeli ko yɛ waa la vooroŋ ane nyɔvore.

ref: But some of you refuse to have faith in me.” Jesus said this, because from the beginning he knew who would have faith in him. He also knew which one would betray him.
text: Kyɛ noba mine be la yɛ poɔ a ba sage de.” Bonso, a Yeezu da baŋɛɛ la a piiluu saŋ banaŋ naŋ da ba sage de a O yɛlɛ ane neɛ na naŋ na yi O puori.

ref: Then Jesus said, “You cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.”
text: Ka O da yeli, “A ŋaa zuiŋ la ka N da yeli ko yɛ ka neɛzaa kontoɔ̃ wa N zie, ka a Saa ba ko o soba sori.”

ref: Because of what Jesus said, many of his disciples turned their backs on him and stopped following him.
text: A ŋaa zuiŋ a O potuuribo yaga da leɛ la puori a ba la tuuro O.

ref: Jesus then asked his twelve disciples if they also were going to leave him.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da soore a O potuuribo pie ne bayi na, “Yɛ meŋ boɔrɔ la ka yɛ leɛ gaa be?”

ref: Simon Peter answered, “Lord, there is no one else that we can go to! Your words give eternal life.
text: Ka Saamon Piita da yeli sɔge O, “Daana, aŋ zie ka te pãã na gaa? Fõõ la taa a nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo yelbie.

ref: We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.”
text: Te sage deɛ la kyɛ baŋ ka fõõ la a Naaŋmen Nensoŋ.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “I chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge ba, “Maa naane kaa-iri a yɛ pie ne bayi be? Kyɛ a yɛ kaŋ waa la sebɛlbɛllɛ.”

ref: Jesus was talking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. He would later betray Jesus, even though he was one of the twelve disciples.
text: A Yeezu da yele kyaare ne la Gyudase, a Saamon Ezekarɔte bidɔɔ. Aneazaa ka a Gyudase da waa la a potuuribo pie ne bayi na kaŋ, kyɛ ona la na yi a Yeezu puori.

ref: Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him.
text: A ŋaa puoriŋ, Yeezu da yɔ yoɔrɔ la a Galili paaloŋ. O da ba sage ka O yɔ a Gyudiya poɔ, bonso, a Gyuumine da boɔrɔ la vuo ka ba ko O.

ref: It was almost time for the Festival of Shelters,
text: A Gyuumine Sege Tigri da tara la.

ref: and Jesus' brothers said to him, “Why don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing.
text: Lɛ la ka O yɔɔmine da yeli ko O, “Yi a kyɛ a gaa Gyudiya poɔ ka a fo potuuribo meŋ te nyɛ a toma fo naŋ tonɔ.

ref: No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!”
text: Bonso, neɛzaa naŋ boɔrɔ ka noba yaga baŋ o yɛlɛ ba maŋ erɛ o yɛlɛ sɔglɔ. Ka fõõ wa erɛ a yɛlɛ ama, de fo meŋɛ wuli a tendaa deme zaa.”

ref: Jesus answered, “My time hasn't yet come, but your time is always here.
text: A Yeezu da yeli ko ba la, “A N saŋ naŋ ba ta sɛre, kyɛ yɛnee eŋ a yɛ saŋ maŋ ta la tegitegilɛ.

ref: The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things.
text: A tendaa noba koŋ baŋ kyiiri yɛ, kyɛ ba kyiiri ma la, N naŋ dire danseɛ ka ba yel-erre waa la faa zuiŋ.

ref: Go on to the festival. My time hasn't yet come, and I am not going.”
text: Yɛnee yɛ gɛrɛ a tigri zie. Maa eŋ ba gɛrɛ a tigri ŋa zie, bonso, a N saŋ naŋ ba ta sɛre.”

ref: Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee. Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
text: O naŋ da wa yeli a ŋaa baare, O da naŋ kyɛnɛ be la a Galili poɔ. Yeezu Naŋ Be A Sigi Tigri Zie

ref: After Jesus' brothers had gone to the festival, he went secretly, without telling anyone.
text: Kyɛ a Yeezu yɔɔmine naŋ da wa gaa a tigri zie baare, O meŋ pãã da sɔgle gaa la a be.

ref: During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, “Where is he?”
text: A Gyuumine da yɔ boɔrɔ la a Yeezu a tigri zie, a soorɔ, “Yeŋ ka O be?”

ref: The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, “Jesus is a good man,” while others were saying, “He is lying to everyone.”
text: A noba yaga da zɔɔrɔ la yɛlɛ a O zuiŋ. A noba mine da yelee la, “O e la nemmeŋɛ,” ka ba mine meŋ yele, “Ai, O bɛllɛ la a noba.”

ref: But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
text: Kyɛ neɛzaa da ba sage yeli O yɛlɛ ka a yi, bonso, ba da zoro la a Gyuumine dabeɛ̃.

ref: When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching.
text: A tigri naŋ da wa te kpɛ sogɔ, Yeezu da te kpɛ la a Naaŋmen die poɔ a piili kanna a noba.

ref: The leaders were surprised and said, “How does this man know so much? He has never been taught!”
text: A Gyuumine nɔɛ da maaɛ la ka ba da soorɔ, “Wola ka a dɔɔ ŋa e baŋ yɛlɛ ŋaa, kyɛ ka neɛzaa daŋ ba wuli O?”

ref: Jesus replied: I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge ba, “A yɛlɛ N naŋ kanna ba waa N meŋɛ yɛlɛ, kyɛ a yi la a soba na naŋ toŋ ma zie.”

ref: If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me.
text: Neɛ na zaa naŋ sereŋ boɔrɔ ka o erɛ Naaŋmen poboɔbo na baŋ ka a N kannoo yi la Naaŋmen zie bee ka N meŋɛ kannoo la.

ref: If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie.
text: Banaŋ naŋ maŋ yele bamenne yɛlɛ maŋ boɔrɛɛ puubu korɔ bamenne, kyɛ neɛ naŋ boɔrɔ puubu korɔ a soba na naŋ toŋ o, ona la taa yelmeŋɛ, ka zirii zaa ba kye o zie.

ref: Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
text: “Moozul mɔɔ naane ko yɛ a Naaŋmen begɛ be? Kyɛ ka yɛ kanzaa ba tuuro a begɛ. Boŋ la so ka yɛ boɔrɔ vuo ka yɛ ko ma?”

ref: The crowd replied, “You're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?”
text: Ka a noba da yeli sɔge, “Fo taa la sefaa! Nembuo la boɔrɔ fo koobo?”

ref: Jesus answered: I worked one miracle, and it amazed you.
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge ba, “Toŋyeni yoŋ ka N toŋ kyɛ ka yɛ zaa nɔɛ maa.

ref: Moses commanded you to circumcise your sons. But it wasn't really Moses who gave you this command. It was your ancestors, and even on the Sabbath you circumcise your sons
text: Moozul la da ko yɛ a bidɔbɔ dɛgre iruu noɔre , ka yɛ pãã maŋ iri a dɔɔ dɛgre a pɛnnoo bebiri daare.

ref: in order to obey the Law of Moses. Why are you angry with me for making someone completely well on the Sabbath?
text: Ka yɛnee maŋ iri bidɔbɔ dɛgre a pɛnnoo bebiri daare, a na tu a Moozul noɔre ŋa, boŋ la so ka yɛ suuri iri ne ma N naŋ saŋ dɔɔ kaŋ baaloŋ a pɛnnoo bebiri daare?

ref: Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, “Isn't this the man they want to kill?
text: Pampana a Gyɛruzɛlɛm noba mine da yele la, “A dɔɔ ŋa naane ka ba boɔrɔ ka ba ko be?

ref: Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah?
text: Kyɛ Ona la be kyɛ a yele yɛlɛ noba poɔ, kyɛ ka ba ba yeli yelzaa ko O! A nembɛrɛ mɔɔ tɔɔ wa baŋ ka a Mɛzaaya la a ŋa wɛ?

ref: But how could that be? No one knows where the Messiah will come from, but we know where this man comes from.”
text: Kyɛ te baŋ la zie a dɔɔ ŋa naŋ yi, kyɛ ka a Mɛzaaya wa wa, neɛzaa koŋ baŋ zie O naŋ yi wa.”

ref: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he shouted, “Do you really think you know me and where I came from? I didn't come on my own! The one who sent me is truthful, and you don't know him.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da yeli ne kɔkɔkpeɛŋaa O naŋ da kanna a noba a Naaŋmen die poɔ, “Yɛ baŋ ma la ane zie N naŋ yi. N meŋɛ waabo naane ka N wa, kyɛ a neɛ na naŋ toŋ ma e la yelmeŋɛ soba, kyɛ ka yɛ ba baŋ O.

ref: But I know the one who sent me, because I came from him.”
text: N baŋ O la, bonso O zie la ka N yi wa, Ona la toŋ ma.”

ref: Some of the people wanted to arrest Jesus right then. But no one even laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
text: Lɛ la ka ba da mɔ ka ba nyɔge O, kyɛ neɛzaa da ba de o nu seene O, a O saŋ naŋ da naŋ ba ta sɛre zuiŋ.

ref: A lot of people in the crowd put their faith in him and said, “When the Messiah comes, he surely won't perform more miracles than this man has done!” Officers Sent To Arrest Jesus
text: Kyɛ noba yaga naŋ da be a nembeɛoŋ poɔ da sage de la a O yɛlɛ a da yele, “Ka a Mɛzaaya wa te wa, O na e la yelwulii a gaŋ a dɔɔ ŋa naŋ e be?” Ba Naŋ Toŋ Nembɛrɛ Ka Ba Te Nyɔge A Yeezu

ref: When the Pharisees heard the crowd arguing about Jesus, they got together with the chief priests and sent some temple police to arrest him.
text: A Faraziimine da woŋ la a nembeɛoŋ naŋ waala yele a Yeezu yɛlɛ, ka a Naaŋmen bagmaalkare ane a Faraziimine da toŋ a Naaŋmen die polisiri ka ba te nyɔge O.

ref: But Jesus told them, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I will return to the one who sent me.
text: Lɛ la ka a Yeezu da yeli, “N koŋ la be a yɛ zie ka a kɔɔre yaga kyɛ ka N leɛ gaa a soba na naŋ toŋ ma zie.

ref: You will look for me, but you won't find me. You cannot go where I am going.”
text: Yɛ na bɔ ma la kyɛ yɛ koŋ nyɛ ma; bonso, a zie N naŋ na be yɛ kontoɔ̃ wa be.”

ref: The people asked each other, “Where can he go to keep us from finding him? Is he going to some foreign country where our people live? Is he going there to teach the Greeks?
text: A Gyuumine da soorɔ la taa, “Yeŋ la ka a dɔɔ ŋa boɔrɔ ka O gaa ka te koŋ baŋ nyɛ O? O mɔɔ boɔrɔ la ka O gaa a Gyuumine naŋ yaare be a Giriki noba poɔ a te kanne a Giriki noba be?

ref: What did he mean by saying that we will look for him, but won't find him? Why can't we go where he is going?” Streams of Life-Giving Water
text: A yɛlɛ ŋa O naŋ yele tɛgɛ la boŋ, ‘Yɛ na bɔ ma la kyɛ yɛ koŋ nyɛ ma’ kyɛ ‘zie na N naŋ be, yɛ koŋ baŋ wa be’?” Bare Naŋ Taa Nyɔvore Koɔ̃

ref: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, “If you are thirsty, come to me and drink!
text: A tigri bebi-baaraa daare naŋ e a tigri bebi-kpoŋ, a Yeezu da iri are la a be a yeli yɛlɛ ne kɔkɔkpeɛŋaa, “Yɛ vɛŋ ka neɛzaa kɔŋnyuuri naŋ kpɛ wa N zie,

ref: Have faith in me, and you will have life-giving water flowing from deep inside you, just as the Scriptures say.”
text: ka soba na naŋ sage de N yɛlɛ wa nyu. Aŋa lɛ a gansoŋ naŋ da yeli, ‘A yelderebɛ sukyie poɔ la ka bare naŋ taa nyɔvore koɔ̃ na zoro yire.’ ”

ref: Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory. The People Take Sides
text: A ŋaa ka O da yeli kyaare ne a Vooroŋ Soŋ, Ona ka a yelderebɛ naŋ be O poɔ na nyɛ. Kyɛ a Vooroŋ Soŋ da naŋ ba wa sɛre, a Yeezu naŋ da naŋ ba nyɛ a O puubu zuiŋ. Wɛltaa Naŋ Kpɛ A Noba Poɔ

ref: When the crowd heard Jesus say this, some of them said, “He must be the Prophet!”
text: Ba naŋ da wa woŋ a yelbie ama, a noba mine da yelee la, “A Ŋmen-yelmanna sereŋ la a ŋa.”

ref: Others said, “He is the Messiah!” Others even said, “Can the Messiah come from Galilee?
