ref: The Scriptures say that the Messiah will come from the family of King David. Doesn't this mean that he will be born in David's hometown of Bethlehem?”
text: A gansoŋ mɔɔ ba yeli ka a Mɛzaaya na e la a Davil bale neɛ kyɛ na yi Bɛtelɛhɛm tembile na poɔ a Davil naŋ da kpeɛrɛ be?”

ref: The people started taking sides against each other because of Jesus.
text: Lɛ la ka wɛltaa da kpɛ a noba sogɔ a Yeezu zuiŋ.

ref: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him. The Leaders Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus
text: A noba mine da boɔrɔ la ka ba nyɔge O, kyɛ neɛzaa da ba de o nu seene O. A Gyuumine Nembɛrɛ Da Ba Sage De a Yeezu Yɛlɛ

ref: When the temple police returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, they were asked, “Why didn't you bring Jesus here?”
text: Lɛ la ka a Naaŋmen die polisiri da leɛ gaa a Naaŋmen bagmaalkare ane a Faraziimine zie, ka ba da soore ba, “Bonso ka yɛ da ba nyɔge O?”

ref: They answered, “No one has ever spoken like this man!”
text: Ka a polisiri da yeli sɔge, “Neɛzaa daŋ ba yeli yɛlɛ aŋa lɛ a dɔɔ ŋa naŋ yeli!”

ref: The Pharisees said to them, “Have you also been fooled?
text: Lɛ la ka a Faraziimine da leɛ yeli, “Serɛzaa O tɔɔ wa bɛlle yɛ meŋ wɛ?

ref: Not one of the chief priests or the Pharisees has faith in him.
text: A nembɛrɛ bee a Faraziimine kanzaa da sage de la a Yeezu yɛlɛ be?

ref: And these people who don't know the Law are under God's curse anyway.”
text: Kyɛ a neŋyaga bama naŋ ba baŋ a Naaŋmen begɛ nyɛ la kaa-emmo.”

ref: Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said,
text: A Nikodemu na naŋ da gaa a Yeezu zie daare kaŋ kyɛ meŋ da waa a Faraziimine kaŋ da soorɛɛ la:

ref: “Our Law doesn't let us condemn people before we hear what they have to say. We cannot judge them before we know what they have done.”
text: “A te bege poɔ te na baŋ yeli ka neɛ le la sɛrɛɛ poɔ, kyɛ ba daŋ ko o soba sori ka o tu o noɔre yɛlɛ ka te baŋ bonna o naŋ e be?”

ref: Then they said, “Nicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.” A Woman Caught in Sin
text: Ka ba leɛ yeli, “Serɛzaa, fo meŋ yi la Galili be? Peɛre nyɛ, fo na nyɛ la ka Ŋmen-yelmanna zaa daŋ ba yi Galili teŋɛ poɔ.” [

ref: but Jesus walked out to the Mount of Olives.
text: A Yeezu da gaa la a Oliva Taŋ zu.

ref: Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
text: A bagu-pipi saŋ, a Yeezu da leɛ wa la a Naaŋmen die poɔ. A noba zaa da wa la O zie, ka O da zeŋ a piili kanna ba.

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses brought in a woman who had been caught in bed with a man who wasn't her husband. They made her stand in the middle of the crowd.
text: A Naaŋmen bege wulwulibo ane a Faraziimine da taa la pɔge kaŋ ba naŋ da nyɔge ka o tonɔ peɛ̃mo toma wa ne, a e ka o are a noba niŋesogɔ,

ref: Then they said, “Teacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband.
text: kyɛ ka ba da yeli ko a Yeezu, “Wulwullo, te nyɔge la a pɔge ŋa ka o tonɔ peɛ̃mo toma.

ref: The Law of Moses teaches that a woman like this should be stoned to death! What do you say?”
text: Kyɛ a bege poɔ, Moozul da tere la noɔre ka te za a pɔge ŋa tɔ ne kubo ko. Pampana wola ka fo meŋ yeli?”

ref: They asked Jesus this question, because they wanted to test him and bring some charge against him. But Jesus simply bent over and started writing on the ground with his finger.
text: Ba da yeli la a ŋaa ka ba e O nyɛ, a pãã na toɔ̃ nyɛ saanaa tuo O. A Yeezu da zuunee la kyɛ sɛgrɛ boma teŋɛŋ ne O nubiri.

ref: They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, “If any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!”
text: Ba naŋ da pullo soorɔ O la ka O da vaa-iri kyɛ yeli ko ba, “Yɛ vɛŋ ka soba na zaa naŋ be yɛ poɔ a ba e yelbieri zaa daŋ de kuuri za ne a pɔge.”

ref: Once again he bent over and began writing on the ground.
text: A tɔntɔbaalɛ ka O da leɛ zuuni a sɛgrɛ boma a teŋɛŋ.

ref: The people left one by one, beginning with the oldest. Finally, Jesus and the woman were there alone.
text: Ba naŋ da wa woŋ a lɛ, ba da leɛ gɛrɛ la, yeni yeni, a piili ne a ba nembɛrɛ, kyɛ te kyɛre a Yeezu yoŋ ne a pɔge ka o are O niŋesogɔ.

ref: Jesus stood up and asked her, “Where is everyone? Isn't there anyone left to accuse you?”
text: A Yeezu da vaa-iree la kyɛ soore a pɔge, “Pɔge, a noba bɛ? Neɛzaa ba nyɛ fo saanaa ka fo seŋ ne la kũũ be?”

ref: “No sir,” the woman answered. Then Jesus told her, “I am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.” Jesus Is the Light for the World
text: Ka a pɔge da yeli, “Daana, neɛzaa ba kyebe.” Ka a Yeezu da yeli, “N meŋ koŋ yeli ka fo taa la saanaa seŋ ne kũũ. Gɛrɛ fo gaabo zie, kyɛ a naŋ yi a ŋaa gɛrɛ, tɔɔ la wa erɛ yelbieri.”] Yeezu La A Andonɛɛ Kyaane

ref: Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, “I am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.”
text: A Yeezu da laboreŋ yeli la yɛlɛ ko a noba, a yele, “Maa la a andonɛɛ kyaane. Soba na zaa naŋ tuuro ma daŋ koŋ baŋ kpɛ lige poɔ, kyɛ o na taa la nyɔvore kyaane.”

ref: The Pharisees objected, “You are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!”
text: Lɛ la ka a Faraziimine da yeli ko O, “Fo leɛ dire la fo meŋɛ danseɛ; a fo danseɛ ba e yelmeŋɛ.”

ref: Jesus replied: Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “Ka maa leɛ dire N meŋɛ danseɛ gba, a N danseɛ e la yelmeŋɛ, bonso, N baŋ la zie na N naŋ yi wa ane zie na N naŋ gɛrɛ. Kyɛ yɛnee ba baŋ zie na N naŋ yi wa bee zie na N naŋ gɛrɛ.

ref: You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone.
text: Yɛ maŋ tu la nensaaloŋ sobie kyɛ di sɛrɛɛ; kyɛ maa eŋ ba dire neɛzaa sɛrɛɛ.

ref: If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me.
text: Ka maa dire noba sɛrɛɛ gba, a N sɛrɛɛdiibu e la yelmeŋɛ, bonso, N yoŋ naane dire a sɛrɛɛ, kyɛ maa ne a N Saa naŋ toŋ ma la dire a sɛrɛɛ.

ref: Your Law requires two witnesses to prove that something is true.
text: A yɛ Naaŋmen begɛ poɔ, ba sɛgɛɛ la ka noba bayi danseɛ la waa yelmeŋɛ.

ref: I am one of my witnesses, and the Father who sent me is the other one.
text: N dire la N meŋɛ danseɛ, ka a N Saa naŋ toŋ ma meŋ dire N danseɛ.”

ref: “Where is your Father?” they asked. “You don't know me or my Father!” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would know my Father.”
text: Lɛ la ka ba soore O, “Yeŋ ka a fo Saa be?” Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge ba, “Yɛ ba baŋ ma bee a N Saa. Ka yɛnee da baŋ ma, yɛ da na baŋ la a N Saa meŋ.”

ref: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
text: A Yeezu da yeli la a yɛlɛ ama O naŋ da are a libie emmo zie a Naaŋmen die poɔ a kanna noba, kyɛ neɛzaa da ba nyɔge O, a O saŋ naŋ da naŋ ba ta sɛre zuiŋ. Yeezu Naŋ Da Daŋ Yeli O Kũũ Yɛlɛ

ref: Jesus also told them, “I am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.”
text: A Yeezu da la yeli ko ba la, “N gɛrɛ la, ka yɛ na bɔ ma, kyɛ yɛ na kpi la a yɛ yelbebe poɔ. Zie na N naŋ gɛrɛ yɛ kontoɔ̃ wa be.”

ref: The people asked, “Does he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?”
text: Lɛ la ka a Gyuumine da yeli, “O mɔɔ boɔrɔ la ka O ko O meŋɛ be? A lɛ mɔɔ la so ka O yele, ‘Zie N naŋ gɛrɛ yɛ kontoɔ̃ wa be’?”

ref: Jesus answered, “You are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Yɛ yi la teŋɛzu, kyɛ ka N yi sazu. Yɛ e la teŋɛzu noba, kyɛ ka maa ba e a teŋɛzu neɛ.

ref: This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.”
text: N da yeli ko yɛ la ka yɛ na kpi la a yɛ yelbebe poɔ, bonso, yɛ sereŋ na kpi la a yɛ yelbebe poɔ ka yɛnee ba sage de ka maa la a soba na.”

ref: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
text: Ka a noba da soore O, “Aŋ la fo?” Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Boŋ mɔɔ la so ka N yele yɛlɛ korɔ yɛ?

ref: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
text: N taa la yɛlɛ yaga na yeli kyaare ne yɛ ane yɛlɛ yaga naŋ naŋ na e ka yɛ le sɛrɛɛ poɔ. Kyɛ a soba na naŋ toŋ ma e la yelmeŋɛ soba, ka a yɛlɛ N naŋ woŋ yi O zie la ka N yele korɔ a teŋɛzu noba.”

ref: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
text: Ba da ba baŋ a tɛgɛ ka a Saa yɛlɛ la ka O da yele korɔ ba.

ref: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
text: Lɛ la ka a Yeezu da yeli, “Ka yɛnee wa te zɛge a Nensaala Bie ka O do saa, lɛ la ka yɛ na baŋ ka maa la a soba na. Yɛ pãã na baŋ ka N meŋɛ yɛlɛ naane ka N da erɛ, kyɛ yɛlɛ na a Saa naŋ ko ma noɔre la ka N da yele.

ref: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
text: Kyɛ a soba na naŋ toŋ ma be la N zie. O ba bare ma N yoŋ, bonso, N maŋ erɛ la yɛlɛ naŋ pɛllɛ O poɔ tegitegilɛ.”

ref: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
text: O naŋ da yele a yɛlɛ ama, noba yaga da sage de la O yɛlɛ. Banaŋ Naŋ Sereŋ E A Yeezu Potuuribo

ref: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da yeli ko a Gyuumine na naŋ da sage de O yɛlɛ, “Ka yɛnee wa sage de a N yɛlɛ a ba leɛrɛ puori, yɛ sereŋ waa la N potuuribo.

ref: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
text: Yɛ na baŋ la a yelmeŋɛ, ka a yelmeŋɛ e ka yɛ so yɛmenne.”

ref: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
text: Ka a noba da leɛ yeli ko O, “Te waa la a Aboraham boorɔ, a daŋ ba e neɛzaa gbaŋgbaare. A fo yɛlɛ ŋa fo naŋ yele, ‘Yɛ na so la yɛmenne tɛgɛ la boŋ’?”

ref: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge ba, “Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, neɛzaa naŋ e yelbieri waa la a yelbieri gbaŋgbaa.

ref: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
text: A gbaŋgbaa ba maŋ taa zie a yiri poɔ tegitegilɛ, kyɛ a yiri bie eŋ maŋ taa la zie a yiri poɔ tegitegilɛ.

ref: If the Son gives you freedom, you are free!
text: Azuiŋ, ka a Bidɔɔ wa e ka yɛ so yɛmenne, yɛ sereŋ na so la yɛmenne.

ref: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
text: N baŋ ka yɛ waa la Aboraham boorɔ, kyɛ ka yɛ boɔrɔ sobiri ka yɛ tu kyɛ ko ma, yɛ naŋ ba taa zie a na ko a N yelbiri a yɛ nyɔvore poɔ zuiŋ.

ref: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
text: Bon N naŋ nyɛ yi a N Saa zie la ka N yele korɔ yɛ, kyɛ yɛnee eŋ, yɛ erɛ bonna yɛ naŋ woŋ yi a Saa zie.” Yeezu Ane Aboraham Yɛlɛ

ref: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
text: Ba da yeli sɔge la a Yeezu, “Aboraham la te Saa.” Ka O da yeli ko ba, “Ka yɛnee da waa Aboraham biiri, yɛ da na erɛ la yɛlɛ na Aboraham naŋ da e.

ref: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
text: Kyɛ pampana ŋa, yɛ moɔrɔ la ka yɛ ko ma, neɛ naŋ yeli a yelmeŋɛ O naŋ woŋ yi a Naaŋmen zie ko yɛ. Ŋaa naane ka Aboraham da e.

ref: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
text: Yɛ sereŋ erɛ la yɛlɛ na a yɛ saa naŋ da maŋ erɛ.” Ka ba da yeli ko a Yeezu, “Te ba waa sɛnsɛmbiiri. Saa yeni yoŋ la ka te taa; Ona la a Naaŋmen meŋɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Ka Naaŋmen da waa yɛ Saa, yɛ da na nɔŋ ma la, bonso, Naaŋmen zie la ka N yi, kyɛ pampana, maa la be kyɛ. N ba wa N meŋɛ waabo, kyɛ Ona la toŋ ma.

ref: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
text: Boŋ la so ka yɛ ba baŋ a yɛlɛ N naŋ yele tɛgɛ? Yɛ naŋ kontoɔ̃ sage de N yɛlɛ la so.

ref: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
text: Yɛ yi la a yɛ saa a sebɛlbɛllɛ zie. A yɛ saa poboɔbo la ka yɛ boɔrɔ ka yɛ e. O da e la neŋkoorɔ a yi a piiluu saŋ zaa, a ba taa yelmeŋɛ, bonso, yelmeŋɛ zaa ba kye o zie. Ka o naŋ wa ŋmaara zirii, o maŋ yele kyaare ne la o meŋɛ waaloŋ, bonso, o e la zirŋmaara ane zirŋmaareba zaa saa.

ref: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
text: Kyɛ N naŋ yele a yelmeŋɛ zuiŋ la so ka yɛ ba sage de a N yɛlɛ.

ref: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
text: Yɛ buo soba la na toɔ̃ wuli ma yelbieri N naŋ e? Ka maa yele a yelmeŋɛ korɔ yɛ, bonso ka yɛ ba sage de a N yɛlɛ?

ref: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
text: Soba na zaa naŋ yi Naaŋmen zie maŋ kyɛlle woŋ la a Naaŋmen yelbie. Yɛ naŋ ba yi Naaŋmen zie la so ka yɛ ba wono a yɛlɛ.”

ref: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
text: A Gyuumine da yeli sɔge la a Yeezu, “A bogriŋ mɔɔ naane ka te yeli ka fo waa la Samareya neɛ kyɛ taa sefaa be?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “N ba taa sefaa, kyɛ N Saa la ka N eŋnɛ, kyɛ ka yɛnee ba eŋnɛ ma.

ref: years old!” they said. “How could you have seen Abraham?”
text: Kyɛ N ba boɔrɔ N meŋɛ puubu. Neɛ kaŋ naŋ waa a sɛredire, ona la boɔrɔ ka N nyɛ puubu.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
text: Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, soba na zaa naŋ tuuro a N yɛlɛ daŋ koŋ kpi.”

ref: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
text: Ka a Gyuumine da yeli ko O, “Ŋaa te pãã baŋ ka fo taa la sefaa. A Aboraham kpie la, ka a Ŋmen-yelmanneba meŋ da kpi, kyɛ ka fo yeli, ‘Neɛzaa naŋ sage de a N yɛlɛ daŋ koŋ kpi.’

ref: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
text: Fo e la kpoŋ gaŋ a te saa Aboraham naŋ kpi be? A Ŋmen-yelmanneba meŋ da kpie la. Fo mɔɔ teɛre ka fo waa la boŋ?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge: Ka maa korɔ N meŋɛ puubu, a N puubu koŋ taa tɛgɛ. A N Saa la korɔ ma puubu. Ona la ka yɛ yeli, ‘O waa la te Naaŋmen,’

ref: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
text: kyɛ ka yɛ ba baŋ O. Kyɛ maa eŋ baŋ O la. Ka maa na yeli ka N ba baŋ O, N na e la zirŋmaara aŋa yɛnee. N baŋ O la, a sagra O yɛlɛ.

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, ba naŋ wa dɔgrɔ Aboraham ka N daŋ bebe la.”

ref: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
text: Lɛ la ka a noba da wuo kubo a na za ne a Yeezu, ka O da sɔgle muri yi a Naaŋmen die poɔ.

ref: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa tɔlɔ gɛrɛ, O da nyɛ la dɔɔ kaŋ ba naŋ da dɔge ka o zɔŋ.

ref: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
text: A Yeezu potuuribo da soore O la, “Wulwullo, a dɔɔ ŋa yelbieri la so ka ba dɔge o ka o zɔŋ bee a o dɔgrebɔ yelbieri la so?”

ref: “No, it wasn't!” Jesus answered. “But because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “A o meŋɛ bee o dɔgrebɔ ba e yelbieri. Ba da dɔge o la ka o zɔŋ ka ka Naaŋmen toma na tu o zie sãã.

ref: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
text: Zie naŋ naŋ waa veɛloŋ a ferɛɛ la ka te toŋ a soba na naŋ toŋ te toma. Zie sɔɔrɔ la ka neɛzaa koŋ la toɔ̃ toŋ toma.

ref: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
text: N naŋ naŋ be a teŋɛzu, maa la a teŋɛzu kyaane”

ref: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa yeli a ŋaa baare, O da toore la nentaare lɔɔ teŋɛ, a bu tɛne ne a nentaare, a de sɔ ne a zɔŋ nimie,

ref: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
text: kyɛ da yeli ko o, “Gaa te pɛge a Silom koɔ̃-puu zie koɔ̃ poɔ” . Lɛ la ka a dɔɔ-zɔŋ da gaa te pɛge o niŋe a da leɛ wa a Yeezu zie, a nyɛrɛ.

ref: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
text: A dɔɔ ŋa laŋkpɛtaaba ane banaŋ naŋ da maŋ nyɛ o ka o sɔrɔ boma da piili soorɔ la taa, “A dɔɔ ŋa naane da maŋ zeŋ sɔrɔ na be?”

ref: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
text: Ka ba mine da yele, “Ona la.” Ka ba mine meŋ da yele, “Ai, ona naane, yitaa la.” Ka a dɔɔ meŋɛ da yele, “Maa la.”

ref: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
text: Ka a dɔɔ da yeli sɔge, “A dɔɔ na ba naŋ boɔlɔ Yeezu la da bu tɛne a de sɔ n nimie, kyɛ da yeli ko ma, ‘Gaa te pɛge a Silom gbɛtɛre koɔ̃ poɔ.’ Lɛ la ka N da gaa te pɛge a deɛ nyɛrɛ.”

ref: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
text: Ka ba da soore a dɔɔ, “Yeŋ ka O be?” Ka a dɔɔ da yeli, “N ba baŋ.” A Faraziimine Naŋ Peɛre A Dɔɔ-zɔŋ Sammo Yɛlɛ

ref: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
text: A pɛnnoo bebiri kaŋ daare la ka a Yeezu da bu a tɛne sɔ ne a dɔɔ-zɔŋ nimie ka a yuori.

ref: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
text: Lɛ la ka a Faraziimine meŋ da piili soorɔ a dɔɔ lɛ o naŋ da e nyɛrɛ. Ka a dɔɔ da yeli ko ba, “A Yeezu la da bu tɛne sɔ a n nimie, ka n da te pɛge ne koɔ̃ a nyɛrɛ.”

ref: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
text: Ka a Farazii noba mine da yeli, “A dɔɔ ŋa ba yi Naaŋmen zie, bonso, O ba tuuro a pɛnnoo bebiri daare tuulo.” Kyɛ ka ba mine meŋ da yeli, “Wola ka neɛ naŋ e yelbe-erɛ na toɔ̃ e a yelwulii ama?” Wɛltaa da kpɛ la a noba poɔ.

ref: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
text: Lɛ la ka a Faraziimine da laboreŋ soore a dɔɔ, “Boŋ ka fo na yeli kyaare ne a dɔɔ ŋa naŋ yuo a fo nimie?” Ka a dɔɔ da yeli, “O waa la Ŋmen-yelmanna.”

ref: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
text: A Gyuumine da ba sage de ka a dɔɔ da e la zɔŋ kyɛ leɛ nyɛrɛ, ka a lɛ vɛŋ ka ba da boɔle o dɔgrebɔ

ref: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
text: a da soore ba, “A yɛ bidɔɔ la a ŋa yɛ naŋ yeli ka yɛ dɔge ne o la zɔnnoŋ be? Wola ka a ta ka o nyɛrɛ pampana ŋa?”

ref: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
text: Ka a o dɔgrebɔ da yeli sɔge, “Te baŋ ka te bie la, ka te laŋ dɔge o ne zɔnnoŋ;

ref: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
text: kyɛ te ba baŋ lɛ a naŋ ta ka o nyɛrɛ pampana ŋa, bee a baŋ neɛ na naŋ yuo a o nimie. Yɛ soore o, o waa la nyembulo. Yɛ vɛŋ ka o yeli o meŋɛ yɛlɛ.”

ref: The leaders called the man back and said, “Swear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.”
text: Azuiŋ ba da leɛ boɔle la a dɔɔ ŋa naŋ da zɔŋ a boyi soba a yeli ko o, “Yeli a yelmeŋɛ Naaŋmen niŋesogɔ! Te baŋ ka a dɔɔ ŋa waa la yelbe-erɛ.”

ref: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
text: Ka a dɔɔ da yeli sɔge, “Maa eŋ ba baŋ ka O waa la yelbe-erɛ bee ka O ba waa. Yelyenaa yoŋ la ka N baŋ. N da zɔŋɛɛ la, kyɛ pampana, N nyɛrɛ la.”

ref: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
text: Ka ba da soore o, “Wola ka O da e fo. Wola ka O da e yuo a fo nimie?”

ref: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text: Ka o da yeli sɔge ba, “N daŋ yeli ko yɛ la kyɛ ka yɛ koŋ kyɛlle woŋ. Boŋ la so ka yɛ la boɔrɔ ka N la maaleŋ manne ko yɛ? Yɛ meŋ boɔrɔ la ka yɛ leɛ O potuuribo be?”

ref: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
text: Lɛ la ka ba da to o, a yele, “Fõõ la waa O potuuro kyɛ tenee eŋ waa la Moozul potuuribo.

ref: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
text: Te baŋ ka Naaŋmen da yeli la yɛlɛ ko Moozul kyɛ a dɔɔ ŋa eŋ, te ba baŋ zie na O naŋ yi wa.”

ref: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
text: Ka a dɔɔ da yeli, “Nɔmaa yeli pãã la ŋa! Yɛ ba baŋ zie na O naŋ yi wa kyɛ Ona la yuo n nimie.

ref: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
text: Te baŋ ka Naaŋmen ba kyɛllɛ yelbe-erebɛ yɛlɛ, kyɛ O maŋ kyɛlle woŋ la neɛ naŋ puoro O kyɛ erɛ O poboɔbo yɛlɛ.

ref: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
text: A yi a teŋɛ piiluu saŋ a wa te ta zenɛ, te daŋ ba woŋ ka neɛzaa yuo la neɛ ba naŋ dɔge ne zɔnnoŋ nimie ka o nyɛrɛ.

ref: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
text: Ka a dɔɔ ŋa da ba yi Naaŋmen zie wa, O da kontoɔ̃ e yelzaa”

ref: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
text: Ka ba da yeli sɔge o, “A fõõ ba naŋ dɔge fo zaa gbuli yelbieri poɔ, fõõ la boɔrɔ ka fo wuli te be?” Ba da digi o la bare a puoruu die poɔ. Seeloŋ Poɔ Zɔnnoŋ

ref: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
text: A Yeezu da woŋ ka ba da digi la a dɔɔ bare a puoruu die poɔ, kyɛ O naŋ da wa nyɛ a dɔɔ, O da soore o la, “Fo sage de la a Nensaala Bie yɛlɛ be?”

ref: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
text: Ka a dɔɔ da yeli sɔge, “Neŋkpoŋ, aŋ la O? Yeli ko ma, lɛ ka N na sage de O yɛlɛ.”

ref: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Fo nyɛ O la. Ona la a soba na naŋ yele a yɛlɛ korɔ fo.”

ref: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
text: Ka a dɔɔ da yeli, “Daana, N sage deɛ la.” O pãã da gbi la dumo a puori a Yeezu ne emmo.

ref: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli, “N wa la a teŋɛ ŋa zu sɛrɛɛdiibu zuiŋ, ka ka banaŋ naŋ ba nyɛrɛ na nyɛrɛ, kyɛ ka banaŋ naŋ nyɛrɛ leɛ zɔnne.”

ref: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko ba, “Ka yɛnee da zɔŋ, yɛ da koŋ taa yelbieri. Kyɛ yɛ naŋ yeli, ‘Te nyɛrɛ la,’ a yɛ yelbebe na kyɛnɛ bebe la.”

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
text: “Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, neɛ na zaa naŋ ba tu a pezage dendɔre kpɛ, kyɛ tu ziyuo do kpɛ, o soba e la nannyige ane fafaara.

ref: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
text: A dendɔ-guuro maŋ yuo la a dendɔre ko o, ka a peere maŋ woŋ a o kɔkɔre. O maŋ boɔle la a o peere kanzaa yuori, a taa a yi ne.

ref: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
text: Ka o naŋ wa taa a o peere zaa yi ne baare, o maŋ de la a weɛ, ka a tuuro o, bonso, a baŋ la o kɔkɔre.

ref: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
text: A koŋ tu saana puori, kyɛ a na zo yi la a saana zie, bonso, a ba baŋ a saana kɔkɔre.”

ref: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
text: Bana la ka Yeezu da yele a yelseŋkpegɛ ŋa terɛ kyɛ ba da ba baŋ bonna O naŋ da yele korɔ ba tɛgɛ.

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep.
text: Lɛ la ka Yeezu da laboreŋ yeli ko ba, “Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, maa la a dendɔre ko a peere.

ref: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
text: A banaŋ zaa naŋ daŋ de N weɛ wa, waa la nannyigri ane fafareba, kyɛ a peere da ba kyɛlle woŋ ba yɛlɛ.

ref: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
text: Maa la a dendɔre. Soba na zaa naŋ tu N zie kpɛ na nyɛ la faabo. O na maŋ kpɛ la kyɛ yi, a nyɛ mɔmaara.

ref: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
text: A nannyige maŋ wa la ka o zu, a ko, a kyɛ sãã yoŋ. Maa eŋ waɛ la ka ka noba na taa nyɔvore, a taa o ka o gore.”

ref: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
text: “Maa la a pekyee-meŋɛ. A pekyee-meŋɛ maŋ de la o nyɔvore biŋ ko a peere.

ref: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
text: Kyɛ pekyeenɛ ba naŋ de ka o kyeenɛ a peere kyɛ ka ba yɔɔrɔ o, ka a peere ba e o soobo, maŋ zo la kyɛ bare a peere ka o naŋ wa nyɛ gboŋgbori ka o waana. A gboŋgbori pãã maŋ nyɔge la a peere ŋmɛyaare.

ref: Hired workers run away because they don't care about the sheep.
text: A pekyeenɛ ba naŋ de maŋ zo la kyɛ bare a peere bonso, o ba taa a peere yɛlɛ.

ref: I am the good shepherd. I know my sheep, and they know me.
text: Maa la a pekyee-meŋɛ. N baŋ la N peere, ka a N peere meŋ baŋ ma,

ref: Just as the Father knows me, I know the Father, and I give up my life for my sheep.
text: aŋa lɛ N Saa naŋ baŋ ma ka N meŋ baŋ O. Kyɛ N de la N nyɔvore tere a N peere zuiŋ.

ref: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
text: N taa la a peere mine naŋ ba e a zage ŋa peere. A ferɛɛ la ka N taa a meŋ wa poɔ a zage ŋa peere poɔ, ka a na kyɛlle woŋ a N kɔkɔre. A lɛ la ka a pãã na e zage yeni peere ane pekyee-yeni.

ref: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
text: A ŋaa zuiŋ la ka a Saa nɔŋ ma, bonso, N de la N nyɔvore tere ka ka N na leɛ de o.

ref: No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
text: Neɛzaa kontoɔ̃ de a N nyɔvore, kyɛ maa meŋɛ la boɔrɔ a de o tere. N taa la kpeɛ̃õ ka N de o tere, kyɛ la taa a kpeɛ̃õ ka N la leɛ de o. N nyɛ la a noɔre ŋa yi a N Saa zie.”

ref: The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them.
text: Wɛltaa da la maaleŋ kpɛ la a Gyuumine sogɔ a Yeezu yelbie ama zuiŋ.

ref: Many of them said, “He has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?”
text: A noba yaga da yele la, “O taa la sefaa ka O zu ba kyebe. Bonso ka yɛ kyɛllɛ o yɛlɛ?”

ref: But others said, “How could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!” Jesus Is Rejected
text: Ka a noba mine meŋ da yele, “A yɛlɛ ama ba e neɛ naŋ taa sefaa yelbie. Sefaa na toɔ̃ yuo la zɔŋ nimie be?” A Gyuumine Naŋ Zagre Yeezu Bare

ref: That winter, Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Temple Festival.
text: A saŋ na poɔ ba da dire la a Naaŋmen die Maaloo tigri. A ɔɔkpoŋ saŋ poɔ da la,

ref: One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch,
text: ka a Yeezu da yɔ kyɛnɛ a Naaŋmen die poɔ, a kyaaraa na ba naŋ da boɔlɔ a Solomɔn Kyaaraa.

ref: and the people gathered all around him. They said, “How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!”
text: Lɛ la ka a Gyuumine da wa ŋmaagyili O kyɛ da soore O, “Fo mɔɔ boɔrɔ la ka te be bonnoo poɔ a kɔɔre seŋ wola? Ka fõõ wa la a Mɛzaaya, yeli weliweli ko te wɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered: I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “N da yeli ko yɛ la kyɛ ka yɛ ba sage de. A toma N naŋ tonɔ a N Saa yuori eŋɛ dire la N danseɛ.

ref: But since you are not my sheep, you don't believe me.
text: Kyɛ yɛ ba sage de, yɛ naŋ ba poɔ a N peere poɔ zuiŋ.

ref: My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me,
text: A N peere maŋ woŋ la a N kɔkɔre. N baŋ a la, ka a maŋ tuuro ma.

ref: and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand.
text: N ko a la nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo, ka a daŋ koŋ kpi zaa. Neɛzaa koŋ baŋ faa a yi ne a N nuuri poɔ.

ref: My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands,
text: Bonna N Saa naŋ ko ma waa la kpoŋ gaŋ ama zaa, ka neɛzaa koŋ baŋ faa o yi a Saa nuuri poɔ.

ref: and I am one with the Father.
text: Maa ne a Saa waa la yeni.”

ref: Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus.
text: A Gyuumine da laboreŋ de la kubo ka ba za ne a Yeezu.

ref: But he said, “I have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?”
text: Ka a Yeezu da soore ba, “N toŋ la tonsonne yaga naŋ yi a Saa zie wuli yɛ. A toma ŋa buo soba eŋɛ la ka yɛ na za ma ne a kubo?”

ref: They answered, “We are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!”
text: Ka a Gyuumine da yeli sɔge, “A ba e a toŋ soŋ fo naŋ toŋ la so ka te na za fo ne kubo, kyɛ fo naŋ to Naaŋmen zone zuiŋ la. Fo waa la nensaala yoŋ, kyɛ de fo meŋɛ boɔlɔ Naaŋmen.”

ref: You can't argue with the Scriptures, and God spoke to those people and called them gods.
text: Ka ba naŋ da boɔlɔ a banaŋ zie a Naaŋmen yelbiri naŋ da wa, ‘ŋmemɛ’, kyɛ ka neɛ kontoɔ̃ leɛre a gansoŋ poɔ yɛlɛ bare,

ref: So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world.
text: wola ka yɛ na baŋ yeli ka a soba na a Saa naŋ maale ka O waa soŋ, ka O toŋ O a teŋɛzu, toorɔ la Naaŋmen zone. N naŋ da yeli, ‘N waa la Naaŋmen Bie zuiŋ’?

ref: If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me.
text: Ka maa ba tonɔ a N Saa toma, ka lɛ yɛ ta sage de N yɛlɛ.

ref: But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
text: Kyɛ ka maa wa tonɔ a N Saa toma, aneazaa ka yɛ ba sage de N yɛlɛ, kyɛ yɛ sage de a N tontonne, ka a lɛ vɛŋ ka yɛ woŋ kyɛ baŋ a tɛgɛ ka a Saa be la N poɔ ka N meŋ be a Saa poɔ.”

ref: and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there,
text: A Yeezu da la leɛ gaŋ gaa la a Gyɔɔdan maŋgaŋne, a lambori benee a Gyɔɔn naŋ da korɔ noba suobu, a da te be a be.

ref: many people came to him. They were saying, “John didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.”
text: Noba yaga da wa la O zie, a da yele, “A Gyɔɔn da ba e yelwulaa zaa, kyɛ yɛlɛ na zaa a Gyɔɔn naŋ da yeli kyaare ne a dɔɔ ŋa waa la yelmeŋɛ.”

ref: A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.
text: Noba yaga da sage de la a Yeezu yɛlɛ a be.

ref: The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
text: Lɛ la ka a Laazoro yɔɔpuuli da toŋ ka ba te yeli a Yeezu, “Daana, a soba na fo naŋ nɔŋ beɛrɛ la.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he said, “His sickness won't end in death. It will bring glory to God and his Son.”
text: Kyɛ Yeezu naŋ da wa woŋ a duoro, O da yelee la, “A baaloŋ ŋa ba e o kũũ baaloŋ, kyɛ o beɛrɛ la ka Naaŋmen yuori na nyɛ puubu, ka a Naaŋmen Bie na tu a be a nyɛ puubu.”

ref: Then he said to his disciples, “Now we will go back to Judea.”
text: A ŋaa puoriŋ la ka a Yeezu da yeli ko a O potuuribo, “Yɛ e ka te la gaa a Gyudiya paaloŋ.”

ref: “Teacher,” they said, “the people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?”
text: A potuuribo da yeli ko O la, “Wulwullo, daare yɛŋyɛŋ ŋa naane ka a Gyuumine da boɔrɔ ka ba za fo ne kubo, kyɛ ka fo la maaleŋ gɛrɛ a be?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Aren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “Gbelli pie ne ayi naane be a ŋmenaa saŋ be? Banaŋ naŋ kyɛnɛ ŋmenaa saŋ ba ŋmeɛrɛ gbɛɛ, bonso, ba maŋ nyɛrɛ la a teŋɛ ŋa zu kyaane.

ref: But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.”
text: Kyɛ banaŋ naŋ kyɛnɛ tensogɔ saŋ maŋ ŋmeɛrɛ la gbɛɛ, bonso, a kyaane ba kye ba poɔ.”

ref: Then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.”
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa yeli a ŋaa baare, O da yeli ko ba la, “A te ba Laazoro gbiree la, kyɛ N gɛrɛ la a be ka N te seŋ o.”

ref: They replied, “Lord, if he is asleep, he will get better.”
text: Ka a potuuribo da yeli ko O, “Daana, ka o naŋ wa gbiri, o na leɛ nyɛ la laafeɛ wɛ.”

ref: Jesus really meant that Lazarus was dead, but they thought he was talking only about sleep.
text: A Laazoro kũũ yɛlɛ la ka a Yeezu da yele korɔ a potuuribo kyɛ ka ba da teɛre ka goɔŋ la ka o sereŋ gbiri.

ref: I am glad I wasn't there, because now you will have a chance to put your faith in me. Let's go to him.”
text: Kyɛ a yɛ zuiŋ, a e ma la noɔ ne N naŋ da ba kyebe, ka a lɛ na vɛŋ ka yɛ na sage de a N yɛlɛ. Yɛ e ka te gaa o zie.”

ref: Thomas, whose nickname was “Twin,” said to the other disciples, “Come on. Let's go, so we can die with him.” Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
text: A Tɔmaase ba naŋ da boɔlɔ Yeebaa da yeli ko la a o potuur-taaba, “Yɛ e ka te meŋ gaa, ka te ne O te laŋ kpi.” Yeezu La A Kuuni Poɔ Leɛ-iruu Ane Nyɔvore

ref: When Jesus got to Bethany, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa ta, O da nyɛɛ la ka ba ũũ a Laazoro ka a ta beri anaare.

ref: Bethany was less than three kilometers from Jerusalem,
text: A Bɛtani teŋɛ da peɛle la Gyɛruzɛlɛm, a e ŋa mɛlɛ ayi.

ref: So during the meal Jesus got up, removed his outer garment, and wrapped a towel around his waist.
text: O da iri la a diibu zu a yage O kpar-kpoŋ bare, kyɛ de bɔɔdowaa leŋ O seɛŋ.

ref: He put some water into a large bowl. Then he began washing his disciples' feet and drying them with the towel he was wearing.
text: O pãã da kyiri la koɔ̃ eŋ laa poɔ a piili pɛgrɛ a O potuuribo gbɛɛ kyɛ maŋ de a bɔɔdowaa naŋ leŋ a O seɛŋ a meelɛ ne a ba gbɛɛ.

ref: But when he came to Simon Peter, this disciple asked, “Lord, are you going to wash my feet?”
text: O naŋ da wa ta Saamon Piita zie, a Saamon Piita da yeli ko O la, “Daana, fo boɔrɔ la ka fo pɛge n gbɛɛ be?”

ref: Jesus answered, “You don't really know what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
text: Ka a Yeezu da leɛ yeli, “A pampana, fo ba baŋ bonna N naŋ erɛ, kyɛ a puoriŋ fo na baŋ la a tɛgɛ.”

ref: “You will never wash my feet!” Peter replied. “If I don't wash you,” Jesus told him, “you don't really belong to me.”
text: Ka a Piita yeli ko O, “Fo daŋ koŋ pɛge a n gbɛɛ togitogi zaa.” Ka a Yeezu da leɛ yeli, “Ka maa ba pɛge fo gbɛɛ, fo koŋ la laŋne ma.”

ref: Peter said, “Lord, don't wash just my feet. Wash my hands and my head.”
text: Ka a Piita da yeli ko O, “Daana, ta pɛge n gbɛɛ yoŋ, kyɛ pɛge n nuuri ane n zu meŋ poɔ!”

ref: Jesus answered, “People who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.”
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Neɛ naŋ so koɔ̃ yi, ba la seŋ ka o pɛge o meŋɛ ka o gbɛɛ yoŋ pɛgoo naane. Kyɛ yɛnee eŋ so la koɔ̃ yi, kyɛ yɛ zaa naane so yi.”

ref: Jesus knew who would betray him. That is why he said, “except for one of you.”
text: A Yeezu naŋ da baŋ a soba na naŋ na yi O puori zuiŋ la so ka O da yeli, “Yɛ zaa naane so koɔ̃ yi.”

ref: After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said: Do you understand what I have done?
text: O naŋ da wa pɛge a ba gbɛɛ baare, a su O kparoo kyɛ leɛ gaa a diibu zie, O da soore ba la, “Yɛ baŋ la bonna N naŋ e ko yɛ?

ref: You call me your teacher and Lord, and you should, because that is who I am.
text: Yɛ boɔlɔ ma la Wulwullo ane Daana; a yelmeŋɛ la a lɛ, bonso, a lɛ la ka N waa.

ref: I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you.
text: N e wuli yɛ la ka yɛ meŋ na erɛ a lɛ N naŋ e ko yɛ.

ref: I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them.
text: Yelmeŋɛ zaa la ka N yele korɔ yɛ, tontonema ba ganna ba neŋkpoŋ, ka banaŋ ba naŋ toŋ meŋ ba ganna a soba na naŋ toŋ ba.

ref: I am not talking about all of you. I know the ones I have chosen. But what the Scriptures say must come true. And they say, “The man who ate with me has turned against me!”
text: Yɛ zaa yɛlɛ naane ka N yele. N baŋ la a banaŋ N naŋ kaa-iri. Kyɛ a eɛɛ la ka a gansoŋ poɔ yɛlɛ na tu a tuubu, ‘A soba na naŋ di a N diibu la leɛ de o gbɛre teɛ ne ma.’

ref: I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am.
text: N yele la a ŋaa korɔ yɛ pampana ŋa, ka ka a saŋ na anaŋ wa e, ka yɛ na sage de ka maa la lɛ.

ref: So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, “Lord, which one of us are you talking about?”
text: Lɛ la ka a potuuro na da tage gbɔge a Yeezu kyɛ soore O, “Daana, nembuo la?”

ref: After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, “I tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.”
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa yeli a ŋaa baare, o teɛroŋ da dɔɔnɔ O la ka O da yeli, “Yelmeŋɛ zaa la ka N yele korɔ yɛ, yɛ kaŋ na yi la N puori a de ma tere.”

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal,
text: A Yeezu potuuro na O naŋ da nɔŋ yaga da zeŋ peɛle la a Yeezu.

ref: and Simon motioned for this disciple to find out which one Jesus meant.
text: Ka a Saamon Piita da kagre o ka o soore a Yeezu nembuo naŋ la ka O yele.

ref: But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor.
text: Ka ba mine da teɛre ka a Gyudase naŋ da e a ba libi-binne la so ka Yeezu da yele korɔ o, “Te da a boma te naŋ na di ne a tigri”; bee, ka o ko a nembaalba bonkaŋ.

ref: Judas took the piece of bread and went out. It was already night. The New Command
text: Azuiŋ, a Gyudase naŋ da wa de a boroboro baare, a tɔntɔbaalɛ ka o da te yi yeŋ. Zie da sɔgɛɛ la. A Nɔpaalaa Yɛlɛ

ref: Then, after God is given glory because of him, God will bring glory to him, and God will do it very soon.
text: Ka Naaŋmen wa nyɛ puubu O zie, a Naaŋmen na ko O la puubu O meŋɛ zie, a de ko O daadaalɛ.

ref: My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, “You cannot go where I am going.”
text: N bibiiri, N koŋ la be yɛ zie ka a la kɔɔre yaga. Yɛ na bɔ ma la, kyɛ a ŋa N naŋ da yeli ko a Gyuumine lɛ, a lɛ ka N yele korɔ yɛ, ‘A zie N naŋ gɛrɛ, yɛ koŋ baŋ wa be.’

ref: Peter asked, “Lord, why can't I go with you now? I would die for you!”
text: Ka a Piita da soore O, “Daana, bonso ka N kontoɔ̃ tu fo pampana ŋa? N na sage kpi la fo zuiŋ.”

ref: “Would you really die for me?” Jesus asked. “I tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.”
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “Fo na sage kpi la a N zuiŋ yaa? Yelmeŋɛ zaa la ka N yele korɔ fo, nɔdaa naŋ koŋ koŋ sɛre kyɛ ka fo kyiiri gbɛre bota ka fo ba baŋ ma.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “Don't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me.
text: “Yɛ ta vɛŋ ka yɛ sukyie dɔɔnɔ yɛ. Yɛ sage de Naaŋmen yɛlɛ, a sage de N meŋ yɛlɛ.

ref: There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you.
text: A N Saa yiri poɔ deri yaga bebe la. Ka a naŋ da ba waa lɛ, yɛ teɛre ka N da na baŋ yeli ko yɛ la ka N gɛrɛ la ka N te nare zie bare yɛ be?

ref: After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together.
text: Ka maa wa gaa te nare a zie bare yɛ, N na leɛ wa taa yɛ la gaa ne a zie N naŋ be, ka yɛ ne ma te be a be.

ref: You know the way to where I am going.”
text: Kyɛ yɛ baŋ la a sori na naŋ gɛrɛ a zie N naŋ gɛrɛ.”

ref: Thomas said, “Lord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?”
text: Ka Tɔmaase da soore a Yeezu, “Daana, te ba baŋ a zie fo naŋ gɛrɛ. Wola ka te na e baŋ a sori naŋ gɛrɛ a be?”

ref: “I am the way, the truth, and the life!” Jesus answered. “Without me, no one can go to the Father.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli ko o, “Maa la a sori, ane a yelmeŋɛ, ane a nyɔvore. Neɛzaa kontoɔ̃ wa a Saa zie, ka a soba ba tu N zie.

ref: If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.”
text: Ka yɛnee baŋ ma, yɛ na baŋ la a Saa meŋ. A yi ŋaa kyɛ gɛrɛ, yɛ baŋ O la kyɛ nyɛ O meŋ.”

ref: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father. That is all we need.”
text: A Filipi da yeli ko la a Yeezu, “Daana, wuli te a Saa kyɛ a na seŋ te la.”

ref: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
text: Ka a Yeezu da soore o, “Filipi, N naŋ be a yɛ zie te kɔɔre seŋ a ŋaa, kyɛ ka yɛ naŋ ba baŋ ma? Neɛ na zaa naŋ nyɛ ma nyɛ la a Saa. Wola ka fo na yeli, ‘Wuli te a Saa’?

ref: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
text: Yɛ ba sage de ka N be la a Saa poɔ, ka a Saa meŋ be N poɔ be? A yelbie N naŋ yele korɔ yɛ ba e maa meŋɛ yɛlɛ ka N yele, kyɛ a Saa naŋ be a N poɔŋ la tonɔ O toma.

ref: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
text: Yɛ sage de a N yɛlɛ ka N be la N Saa poɔ, ka a Saa meŋ be a N poɔ. Ka yɛnee ba sage de a N yelbie gba, kyɛ yɛ kaa a N toma kyɛ sage de.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
text: Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, soba na zaa naŋ sage de N yɛlɛ, o meŋ na toŋ la a toma N naŋ tonɔ. A soba sereŋ na toŋ la tombɛrɛ yaga gaŋ a ama, bonso, N gɛrɛ la a Saa zie.

ref: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
text: Bonna zaa yɛ naŋ na sɔre a N yuori eŋɛ, N na e ko yɛ la ka a Saa na nyɛ puubu a Bidɔɔ zie.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
text: Ka yɛnee wa nɔŋ ma, yɛ na sagra la a N nɔɛ.

ref: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
text: Kyɛ N na sɔre la a Saa ka O ko yɛ Soŋnɔ ka O wa be yɛ zie tegitegilɛ.

ref: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
text: Ona la a yelmeŋɛ Vooroŋ. Ona la ka a teŋɛzu deme kontoɔ̃ nyɛ bonso, ba ba nyɛrɛ O bee a baŋ O. Yɛnee eŋ baŋ O la, bonso, O kpeɛrɛ ne yɛ la, kyɛ na be yɛ poɔ.

ref: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
text: “N koŋ bare yɛ ka yɛ waa bikpeere. N leɛ waana la yɛ zie.

ref: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
text: A koŋ la kɔɔre yaga kyɛ ka a teŋɛzu deme koŋ la nyɛ ma, kyɛ yɛnee na nyɛ ma la, N naŋ voorɔ zuiŋ yɛ meŋ na voorɔ la.

ref: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: A lɛ daare yɛ na baŋ ka N be la a N Saa poɔ, ka yɛ be N poɔ, kyɛ ka N meŋ be yɛ poɔ.

ref: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
text: Banaŋ naŋ taa a N nɔɛ kyɛ tuuro a la nɔŋ ma. Kyɛ a N Saa na nɔŋ la banaŋ naŋ nɔŋ ma, ka N meŋ nɔŋ ba kyɛ de N meŋɛ wuli ba.”

ref: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, “Lord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?”
text: Lɛ la ka Gyudase yeli ko O, “Daana, boŋ la so ka fo na de fo meŋɛ wuli te kyɛ koŋ de wuli a teŋɛzu noba?”

ref: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge o, “Banaŋ naŋ nɔŋ ma na tuuro la N yɛlɛ. A N Saa na nɔŋ ba la ka te wa laŋ kpeɛrɛ ne ba.

ref: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
text: Soba na zaa naŋ ba nɔŋ ma, ba tuuro N yɛlɛ. Kyɛ a yelbie yɛ naŋ wono ka a yire N zie ba e N meŋɛ yelbie, kyɛ a yi la a Saa naŋ toŋ ma zie.”

ref: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
text: “N da yeli la a yɛlɛ ama ko yɛ N naŋ da naŋ kyɛnɛ be a yɛ zie.

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
text: Kyɛ a yɛ Soŋnɔ, a Vooroŋ Soŋ, Ona la ka a Saa na toŋ a yɛ zie N yuori eŋɛ. O na wa wuli yɛ la yelzaa kyɛ leɛ-teɛre yɛ a yɛlɛ na zaa N naŋ da yeli ko yɛ.

ref: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
text: N bara la emmaaroŋ korɔ yɛ. N korɔ yɛ la N emmaaroŋ. N ba korɔ yɛ aŋa lɛ a teŋɛzu deme naŋ maŋ terɛ lɛ. Yɛ ta vɛŋ ka yɛ sukyie dɔɔnɔ yɛ bee ka dabeɛ̃ kpɛ yɛ.

ref: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
text: Yɛ woŋee la ka N da yeli ko yɛ, ‘N gɛrɛ la kyɛ na leɛ wa yɛ zie.’ Ka yɛnee da nɔŋ ma, yɛ da na kyellɛ la ne N naŋ gɛrɛ a Saa zie, bonso, a Saa taa la kpeɛ̃õ gaŋ ma.

ref: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
text: Kyɛ pampana, N yeli la a yɛlɛ ŋa ko yɛ kyɛ ka a na wa e, ka ka anaŋ wa e, ka yɛ na sage de.

ref: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
text: N koŋ la yeli yɛlɛ yaga ne yɛ, bonso, a teŋɛ ŋa zu naa waana la. O ba taa kpeɛ̃õ N zu.

ref: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
text: Kyɛ N erɛ la lɛ N Saa naŋ eŋ noɔre ko ma ka ka a teŋɛzu noba na baŋ ka N nɔŋ la a Saa. Yɛ iri ka te gaa.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
text: “Maa la a dããteɛ meŋɛ, kyɛ ka N Saa e a dããteɛ te-sɛllɛ.

ref: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
text: O maŋ kyɛ la ulee na zaa naŋ be N eŋɛ kyɛ ba wɔnɔ wɔmɔ bare. Ulee na zaa naŋ wɔnɔ wɔmɔ, O maŋ gbɛnne o la ka o na wɔŋ yaga.

ref: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
text: A N yelbiri N naŋ da yeli ko yɛ pɛge yɛ la ka yɛ yi baare.

ref: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
text: Yɛ be N poɔ aŋa lɛ N naŋ be yɛ poɔ. Aŋa lɛ teɛ ulee naŋ koŋ baŋ wɔŋ wɔmɔ ka o naŋ ba be a teɛ eŋɛ, a lɛ meŋ la ka yɛ kontoɔ̃ wɔŋ wɔmɔ ka yɛnee ba be N eŋɛ.

ref: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
text: Maa la a dããteɛ, kyɛ ka yɛ waa a ulli. Banaŋ naŋ be N poɔ ka N meŋ be ba poore maŋ wɔŋ la wɔmɔ yaga, bonso, ka yɛnee ba be N eŋɛ, yɛ kontoɔ̃ e yelzaa.

ref: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
text: Soba na zaa naŋ ba be N poɔ, ba na lɔɔ o la bare aŋa teɛ ulee ka o ko. A ulli ama taaba, ba na wuo a la lantaa a lɔɔ eŋ vũũ poɔ ka a di.

ref: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
text: Ka yɛnee wa be N poɔ, ka a N yelbie be yɛ poore, yɛ sɔre bonzaa yɛ naŋ boɔrɔ, kyɛ ba na e ko yɛ la.

ref: When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored.
text: Ka yɛnee wɔnɔ wɔmɔ yaga kyɛ waa N potuuribo, ŋaa la na e ka N Saa yuori na nyɛ puubu.

ref: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
text: Aŋa lɛ a Saa naŋ nɔŋ ma, lɛ la ka N meŋ nɔŋ yɛ. Yɛ be a N nɔmmo poɔ.

ref: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
text: Ka yɛnee wa sagra a N nɔɛ, yɛ na be la a N nɔmmo poɔ, aŋa lɛ N naŋ sagra a N Saa nɔɛ a be a O nɔmmo poɔ lɛ.

ref: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
text: N da yeli la a yɛlɛ ama ko yɛ, ka N kyelloo na be yɛ zie, ka a yɛ kyelloo na nyɛ paaloo.”

ref: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
text: “A N noɔre la ŋa: yɛ nɔnɔ taa aŋa lɛ N naŋ da nɔŋ yɛ.

ref: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
text: Neɛzaa ba taa nɔmmo ka o gaŋ a ŋaa: ka neɛ na sage kpi o bamine zuiŋ.

ref: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
text: Ka yɛnee wa sagra N nɔɛ, yɛ waa la N bamine.

ref: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
text: N ba la kyɛnɛ boɔlɔ yɛ tontonema, bonso, tontonɔ ba maŋ baŋ bon a o neŋkpoŋ naŋ erɛ. Kyɛ N boɔlɔ yɛ la N bamine bonso, N eɛɛ la ka yɛ baŋ yɛlɛ na zaa N naŋ da woŋ yi a N Saa zie.

ref: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: Yɛnee naane kaa-iri ma, kyɛ maa la kaa-iri yɛ, a toŋ yɛ ka yɛ gaa te wɔnɔ wɔmɔ. Wɔmɔ na naŋ na bebe kɔɔre, ka a Saa pãã na ko yɛ bonna zaa yɛ naŋ sɔre yi O zie a N yuori eŋɛ.

ref: So I command you to love each other. The World's Hatred
text: N korɔ yɛ la a nɔɛ ama ka ka yɛ na nɔnɔ taa.” A Tendaa Noba Na Kyiiri Yɛ La

ref: If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first.
text: “Ka a tendaa noba wa kyiire yɛ, yɛ baŋ velaa ka maa la ka ba da daŋ kyiiri kyɛ pãã wa kyiire yɛ.

ref: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
text: Ka yɛnee da poɔ a tendaa noba poɔ, ba da na nɔŋ yɛ la aŋa bamenne noba. N naŋ kaa-iri yɛ yi ne a tendaa noba poɔ, ka yɛ ba la poɔ ba poɔ la so ka a tendaa noba kyiire yɛ.

ref: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
text: Yɛ leɛ-teɛre a yelbiri na N naŋ da yeli ko yɛ, ‘Tontonema ba ganna ba neŋkpoŋ.’ Ka ba naŋ da dɔgre ma, ba na dɔgre la yɛ meŋ. Ka ba naŋ da sage de a N yɛlɛ, ba na sage de la yɛ meŋ yɛlɛ.

ref: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
text: Kyɛ ba na e yɛ la a ama zaa a N yuori zuiŋ, bonso, ba ba baŋ a soba na naŋ toŋ ma.

ref: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
text: Ba da koŋ taa yelsaanaa zaa ka maa da ba wa yeli yɛlɛ ko ba. Kyɛ ŋaa eŋ, ba ba taa gaafara zaa na tere kyaare ne a ba yelbebe.

ref: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
text: Soba na zaa naŋ kyiiri ma, kyiiri la N Saa meŋ.

ref: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
text: Ka maa da ba wa toŋ a toma ama neɛzaa naŋ daŋ ba toŋ zaa ba poɔ, ba da koŋ taa yelbieri zaa. Kyɛ pampana, ba nyɛɛ la kyɛ naŋ kyiiri ma ane a N Saa.

ref: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, “People hated me for no reason.”
text: A yɛlɛ ama eɛɛ la ka ka a na tu a yelbiri ba naŋ da sɛge eŋ a Naaŋmen begɛ poɔ, ‘Ba kyiiri ma la kyɛ ka N ba sãã yelzaa.’ ”

ref: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
text: “Ka a Soŋnɔ, a yelmeŋɛ Vooroŋ N naŋ na toŋ ka O yi a Saa zie wa, na wa di la N danseɛ.

ref: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
text: Yɛ meŋ na di la a N danseɛ, bonso, yɛ da be la N zie a yi a piiluu saŋ.”

ref: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
text: “N da yeli la a yɛlɛ ama ko yɛ ka ka a na gu yɛ ka yɛ ta wa ŋmɛ gbɛre le.

ref: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
text: Ba na digi yɛ la bare a puoruu deri poore. Serɛzaa, a saŋ waana la ka noba na ko yɛ kyɛ maŋ teɛre ka Naaŋmen la ka ba puoro.

ref: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
text: Ba na e la a ŋaa ba naŋ ba baŋ a Saa bee maa zuiŋ.

ref: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
text: Kyɛ N da yeli la a yɛlɛ ama ko yɛ ka ka a ba saŋ wa ta, ka yɛ na leɛ-teɛre baŋ ka N da daŋ yeli la a ba yɛlɛ ko yɛ.” A Vooroŋ Soŋ Toma Yɛlɛ “N da ba yeli a yɛlɛ ama ko yɛ a piiluu saŋ, bonso, N da be la yɛ zie.

ref: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
text: Kyɛ pampana ŋa N leɛ gɛrɛ la a soba na naŋ toŋ ma zie, kyɛ ka yɛ kanzaa ba soore ma, ‘Yeŋ ka fo gɛrɛ?’

ref: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
text: Kyɛ N naŋ da yeli a yɛlɛ ama ko yɛ zuiŋ yɛ poore da sããɛ la.

ref: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
text: Kyɛ a yelmeŋɛ la ka N yele korɔ yɛ: A na e la velaa ko yɛ ka N gaa, bonso, ka maa ba gaa, a yɛ Soŋnɔ koŋ wa. Kyɛ ka maa wa gaa, N na toŋ O la ka O wa yɛ zie.

ref: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
text: Ka O naŋ wa wa, O na wuli la a tendaa noba ka ba tulee la a kyaare ne ba yelbebe, yelmennoŋ ane sɛrɛɛdiibu yɛlɛ.

ref: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
text: O na wuli ba la kyaare ne ba yelbebe ba naŋ ba sage de N yɛlɛ zuiŋ;

ref: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
text: a kyaare ne yelmennoŋ, bonso, N leɛ gɛrɛ la a Saa zie, ka yɛ koŋ la nyɛ ma.

ref: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
text: O na wuli ba la kyaare ne sɛrɛɛdiibu, bonso, a tendaa ŋa naa sɛrɛɛ di baarɛɛ la.”

ref: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
text: “N naŋ taa la yɛlɛ yaga na yeli ko yɛ, kyɛ yɛ kontoɔ̃ de a zaa pampana.

ref: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
text: Ka a yelmeŋɛ Vooroŋ wa wa, O na wuli yɛ la a yelmeŋɛ zaa, bonso, O koŋ yeli O meŋɛ yɛlɛ, kyɛ yelzaa O naŋ woŋ yi a Saa zie la ka O na yeli ane yɛlɛ na naŋ na e.

ref: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
text: O na puu ma la, bonso, bon naŋ e N soobo la ka O na de wuli yɛ.

ref: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
text: Bonzaa a Saa naŋ taa e la N soobo. Lɛnso ka N da yeli ko yɛ ka O na de la bon naŋ e N soobo a wuli yɛ.” Posãã Na Leɛ La Popeɛloŋ

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “For a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.”
text: “A koŋ kɔɔre kyɛ ka yɛ koŋ la nyɛ ma, kyɛ ka a la e belaa ka yɛ la nyɛ ma.”

ref: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
text: Lɛ la ka a O potuuribo mine da soorɔ taa, “Boŋ la ka O boɔrɔ ka O yeli ko te ne a ŋaa O naŋ yeli, ‘A kyɛre la belaa lɛ ka yɛ koŋ la nyɛ ma, kyɛ ka a la e belaa ka yɛ la nyɛ ma, bonso N gɛrɛ la a Saa zie’?”

ref: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
text: Ba da yelee la, “A ŋaa O naŋ yeli tɛgɛ la boŋ, ‘A kyɛre la belaa lɛ’? Te ba baŋ bonna O naŋ yele kyaare ne.”

ref: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
text: A Yeezu da baŋ ka ba da boɔrɔ la ka ba soore O, lɛnso ka O da soore ba, “Yɛ mɔɔ leɛ soorɛɛ taa N naŋ yeli a ŋaa tɛgɛ be, ‘A koŋ kɔɔre kyɛ ka yɛ koŋ la nyɛ ma, kyɛ ka a la e belaa ka yɛ la nyɛ ma’?

ref: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
text: Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, yɛ na ŋma la nentaŋ kyɛ koŋ, kyɛ a teŋɛzu noba na kyellɛ la. Yɛ na woŋ la tuo, kyɛ a yɛ tuo wommu na leɛ la kyelloo.

ref: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
text: Ka pɔge wa te boɔrɔ dɔgebo, a o poɔ maŋ beɛrɛ la, bonso, a o saŋ taɛ la. Kyɛ ka o naŋ wa te dɔge a bie baare, o ba maŋ la teɛrɛ a tuo o naŋ da woŋ, bonso o maŋ taa la kyelloo ne o naŋ dɔge bie.

ref: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
text: Lɛ meŋ la ka yɛ wono tuo pampana, kyɛ N na leɛ wa nyɛ yɛ la, ka yɛ taa kyelloo yɛ sukyie poɔ. Neɛzaa kontoɔ̃ de a yɛ kyelloo yi yɛ zie.

ref: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: A lɛ daare yɛ koŋ sɔre bonzaa yi N zie. Yelmeŋɛ zaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, ka yɛnee wa sɔre bonzaa yi a Saa zie a N yuori eŋɛ, O na ko yɛ la.

ref: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
text: A wa te ta ŋaa, yɛ naŋ ba sɔre bonzaa N yuori eŋɛ. Yɛ sɔre, kyɛ yɛ na nyɛ la, lɛ la ka yɛ kyelloo na nyɛ paaloo.” Yeezu Potuuribo Emmaaroŋ Yɛlɛ

ref: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
text: “N da lɔɔ la sekpɔgɔ yeli ne yɛlɛ ko yɛ kyɛ a saŋ waana la ka N koŋ la lɔɔ sekpɔgɔ yeli ne yɛlɛ ko yɛ kyɛ N na yeli la a Saa yɛlɛ weliweli lɛ ko yɛ.

ref: You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you.
text: A lɛ daare yɛ na sɔre la a N yuori eŋɛ. N ba yeli ko yɛ ka N na are la yɛ gbɛbogriŋ kyɛ sɔre yi a Saa zie ko yɛ.

ref: God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him.
text: Bonso, a Saa meŋɛ nɔŋ yɛ la, yɛ naŋ nɔŋ ma kyɛ da sage de ka N yi la a Saa zie wa zuiŋ.

ref: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
text: A Saa zie la ka N da yi wa a teŋɛzu, kyɛ N la yire la a teŋɛzu a leɛ gɛrɛ a Saa zie.”

ref: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
text: A O potuuribo da yeli ko O la, “Õõ, ŋaa fo pãã yele la yɛlɛ weliweli korɔ te, a ba la lɔɔrɔ sekpɔgɔ!

ref: At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.”
text: Ŋaa eŋ te pãã baŋ ka fo baŋ la yelzaa, ka a ba fere ka neɛzaa la soore fo yelzaa. A ŋaa zuiŋ la ka te sage de ka fo yi la Naaŋmen zie.”

ref: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
text: Ka a Yeezu da soore ba, “Yɛ pãã sage deɛ la be?

ref: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
text: A saŋ waana la; o taɛ la gba, ka ba na ŋmɛ yɛ yaare bare ka kanzaa zo gaa o yiri kyɛ kyɛre N yoŋ. Kyɛ N ba waa N yoŋ, bonso, a Saa be la N zie.

ref: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
text: N daŋ yeli la a ŋaa ko yɛ ka ka yɛ na taa emmaaroŋ N zie. Yɛ na nyɛ la dɔgroŋ a teŋɛzu, kyɛ yɛ nyɔge yɛmenne, bonso, N zɔɔtoɔ̃ la a tendaa!”

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa yeli a yelbie ama baare, O da gbole kaa la sazu kyɛ yeli, “N Saa, a saŋ taɛ la. Iri a fo Bidɔɔ yuori, ka a fo Bidɔɔ meŋ pãã iri a fo yuori.

ref: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
text: Fo ko O la kpeɛ̃õ nensaalba zaa zu, ka O ko banaŋ zaa fo naŋ ko O nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo.

ref: Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
text: A nyɔvore naŋ ba taa baaroo la ŋa: ka ba baŋ fo yoŋ naŋ waa Naaŋmen meŋɛ ane a Yeezu Kirista fo naŋ toŋ.

ref: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
text: N iri la a fo yuori a teŋɛzu ne a toma fo naŋ ko ma ka N toŋ baare.

ref: Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
text: Pampana ŋa, N Saa, pãã iri N yuori a fo meŋɛ niŋesogɔ ne a yuori na N naŋ da taa fo zie kyɛ ka a tendaa wa bebe.”

ref: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
text: “A noba banaŋ fo naŋ da iri a tendaa poɔ ko ma, N eɛɛ la ka ba baŋ a fo yuori. Ba da waa la fo soobo, kyɛ ka fo da de ba ko ma ka ba da tuuro a fo yelbiri.

ref: They know that you gave me everything I have.
text: Ŋaa ba baŋ ka bonzaa fo naŋ ko ma yi la fo zie,

ref: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
text: bonso, N ko ba la a yelbie na fo naŋ da ko ma. Ba de la a yelbie kyɛ baŋ a yelmeŋɛ ka N yi la fo zie wa. Ba sage deɛ la ka fõõ la toŋ ma.

ref: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
text: A bana zuiŋ la ka N puoro. N ba puoro a tendaa noba zuiŋ, kyɛ banaŋ fo naŋ ko ma zuiŋ la ka N puoro, bonso, fõõ la so ba.

ref: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
text: Banaŋ zaa N naŋ so e la fo soobo, ka banaŋ zaa fo naŋ so meŋ e N soobo, ka N yuori na nyɛ puubu ba zie.

ref: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
text: Kyɛ pampana, N ba la be a teŋɛzu, kyɛ bana eŋ naŋ be la a teŋɛzu kyɛ ka N waana fo zie. N Saa Soŋ, gu ba a fo yuori fo naŋ ko ma zuiŋ, ka ka ba na waa yeni aŋa maa ne fo naŋ waa yeni lɛ.

ref: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
text: N naŋ da be a ba zie, N da gu ba la a fo yuori fo naŋ da ko ma zuiŋ. N da gu ba la ka ba kanzaa da ba bɔre, ka a soba na a naŋ nare biŋ ka o wɛ yoŋ naane, ka ka a gansoŋ poɔ yɛlɛ na tu a tuubu.

ref: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
text: Kyɛ pampana N waana la fo zie. N yele la a yɛlɛ ama a teŋɛzu ka ka N kyelloo na paale ba zie.

ref: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
text: N ko ba la a fo yelbiri, kyɛ a tendaa noba da kyiiri ba la, ba naŋ ba poɔ a tendaa noba poɔ zuiŋ aŋa N meŋ naŋ ba poɔ a tendaa noba poɔ lɛ.

ref: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
text: N ba sɔrɔ ka fo iri ba yi ne a tendaa poɔ, kyɛ N sɔrɔ la ka fo guuro ba a faaloŋ soba eŋɛ.

ref: They don't belong to this world, and neither do I.
text: Ba ba poɔ a tendaa noba poɔ aŋa N naŋ ba poɔ a tendaa noba poɔ lɛ.

ref: Your word is the truth. So let this truth make them completely yours.
text: Maale ba eŋ a yelmeŋɛ poɔ. A fo yelbiri waa la yelmeŋɛ.

ref: I am sending them into the world, just as you sent me.
text: A lɛ fo naŋ da toŋ ma a teŋɛzu, a lɛ la ka N meŋ toŋ ba a teŋɛzu.

ref: I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
text: Kyɛ a ba eŋɛ la ka N maale N meŋɛ, ka ka ba meŋ na maale bamenne a yelmeŋɛ poɔ.”

ref: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
text: “Ba yoŋ eŋɛ naane ka N puoro, kyɛ N puoro korɔ la a banaŋ zaa naŋ na sage de N yɛlɛ a ba yelbiri zuiŋ,

ref: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
text: ka ba zaa na e yeni. N Saa, aŋa fo naŋ be N poɔ ka N meŋ be fo poɔ lɛ, e ka ba meŋ be te poɔ. Lɛ la ka a tendaa noba na sage de ka fõõ la toŋ ma.

ref: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.
text: A puubu na fo naŋ ko ma, N meŋ de ko ba la, ka ba meŋ na e yeni aŋa te naŋ waa yeni lɛ.

ref: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
text: N be la ba poɔ ka fo be N poɔ, ka ka ba na lantaa e yeni. Lɛ la ka a tendaa noba na baŋ ka fõõ la toŋ ma kyɛ nɔŋ ba aŋa fo naŋ nɔŋ ma lɛ.

ref: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
text: N Saa, N nɔnɔ la ka a banaŋ fo naŋ ko ma meŋ na laŋ ne ma a zie N naŋ be, ka ba na nyɛ a puubu fo naŋ ko ma, bonso, fo nɔŋ ma la sɛre kyɛ ka a tendaa wa piili.”

ref: Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me.
text: “Yelmennoŋ Saa, a teŋɛzu noba ba baŋ fo kyɛ maa baŋ fo la, ka a noba bama meŋ baŋ ka fõõ la toŋ ma.

ref: I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
text: N eɛɛ la ka ba baŋ a fo yuori, kyɛ na e ka ba baŋ o. Lɛ la ka a nɔmmo fo naŋ nɔŋ ma na be ba poɔ, kyɛ ka N be ba poɔ.”

ref: One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, “Didn't I see you in the garden with that man?”
text: A Naaŋmen bagmaale wederebɛ nembɛrɛ zusoba gbaŋgbaa kaŋ naŋ da waa a dɔɔ na Piita naŋ da kyɛ o toori kpɛlle dɔgrɔ da soore la a Piita, “N da ba nyɛ fo a gaaden poɔ ne a Yeezu be?”

ref: Jesus again asked, “Who are you looking for?” “We are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,” they answered.
text: A Yeezu da laboreŋ soore ba la, “Aŋ la ka yɛ boɔrɔ?” Ka ba da yeli, “Yeezu naŋ yi Naazɛrɛ.”

ref: This time Jesus replied, “I have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go.
text: Ka a Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “N yeli ko yɛ la ka maa la lɛ. Azuiŋ, ka maa wa la ka yɛ boɔrɔ, yɛ bare a bama ka ba gɛrɛ.”

ref: Then everything will happen, just as I said, ‘I did not lose anyone you gave me.’ ”
text: A ŋaa da eɛɛ la ka a na tu lɛ O naŋ da yeli, “A banaŋ fo naŋ da ko ma, N ba e ka ba kanzaa bɔre.”

ref: Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
text: Lɛ la ka Saamon Piita da voɔ o zɔɔzɔɔ-soɔ o naŋ da taa a kyɛne a Naaŋmen bagmaale wederebɛ nembɛrɛ zusoba gbaŋgbaa duluŋ toori kpɛlle. A gbaŋgbaa yuori da la Malku.

ref: The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up.
text: Lɛ la ka a sogyɛre ane ba neŋkpoŋ ane a Gyuumine polisiri da nyɔge a Yeezu leŋ.

ref: Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in,
text: kyɛ ka Piita da are a yeŋ poɔ, a dendɔre noɔreŋ. Lɛ la ka a potuuro na a Naaŋmen bagmaale wederebɛ nembɛrɛ zusoba naŋ da baŋ da yi te yeli yɛlɛ ko a pɔge na naŋ guuro a dendɔre kyɛ da taa a Piita kpɛ ne.

ref: but asked him, “Aren't you one of that man's followers?” “No, I am not!” Peter answered.
text: A pɔge da soore la Piita, “Fo meŋ ba waa a dɔɔ ŋa potuuro kaŋ be?” Ka a Piita da yeli, “N ba waa kaŋ.”

ref: It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself. Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest (Matthew
text: A gbaŋgbaare ne a polisiri da mare la saala vũũ a are ŋmaagyili uoro, bonso, ɔɔre da erɛ la. A Piita meŋ da are la ba poɔ a uoro a vũũ. A Naaŋmen Bagmaale Wederebɛ Nembɛrɛ Zusoba Naŋ Soore Yeezu Soorebie (Mat

ref: The high priest questioned Jesus about his followers and his teaching.
text: Lɛ la ka a Naaŋmen bagmaale wederebɛ nembɛrɛ zusoba da soore Yeezu kyaare ne a O potuuribo ane a O kannoo yɛlɛ.

ref: Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.”
text: Bonso ka fo soorɔ ma? Soore a banaŋ naŋ woŋ a yɛlɛ N naŋ da yeli ko ba. Bana la baŋ bonna N naŋ yeli.”

ref: Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus (Matthew
text: Lɛ la ka Anaase da e ka ba leŋ a Yeezu, a taa gaa te ko Kayefa, a Naaŋmen bagmaale wederebɛ nembɛrɛ zusoba. Piita Naŋ Laboreŋ Zagre A Yeezu Yɛlɛ (Mat

ref: ) Pilate went back out and said, “I don't find this man guilty of anything!
text: ) A Pilato naŋ da wa yeli a ŋaa baare, O da la yi la a Gyuumine zie a te yeli ko ba, “N ba nyɛ O yelsaanaa zaa.

ref: It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
text: Ba pãã da taa la a Yeezu yi ne a Kayefa zie a gaa ne a Pilato nayikpoŋ poɔ. A da waa la bagu-pipi saŋ. A Gyuumine da ba kpɛ a nayikpoŋ poɔ, ka ka ba ta wa kpɛ dɛgre a na toɔ̃ di a Ista tigri.

ref: Pilate came out and asked, “What charges are you bringing against this man?”
text: Lɛ la ka Pilato da yi wa ba zie a soore ba, “Boŋ yelsaanaa la ka yɛ taa kyaare ne a dɔɔ ŋa?”

ref: They answered, “He is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.”
text: Ka ba da yeli sɔge, “Ka a dɔɔ ŋa da ba waa nenfaa, te da koŋ nyɔge O wa ne a fo zie.”

ref: Pilate then went back inside. He called Jesus over and asked, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
text: Lɛ la ka Pilato da la kpɛ a die poɔ a boɔle a Yeezu a soore O, “Fõõ la a Gyuumine Naa be?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Are you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?”
text: Ka a Yeezu da leɛ soore, “Fo meŋɛ la baŋ a yeli ŋa a soorɔ ma bee noba mine la yeli a N yɛlɛ ko fo?”

ref: “You know I'm not a Jew!” Pilate said. “Your own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?”
text: Ka a Pilato da leɛ yeli, “N mɔɔ waa la Gyuu neɛ be? A fo meŋɛ noba ane a Naaŋmen bagmaale wederebɛ nembɛrɛ la taa fo wa ne a N zie. Boŋ la ka fo e?”

ref: Jesus answered, “My kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.”
text: Ka Yeezu da yeli sɔge, “N naaloŋ ba yi a tendaa ŋa poɔ. Ka a N naaloŋ da yi a tendaa ŋa poɔ, a N potuuribo da na zɔɔrɔ la ka ba faa ma yi ne a Gyuumine nuuriŋ. Kyɛ lɛ anaŋ waa, N naaloŋ ba yi a tendaa ŋa poɔ.”
